volume.h1:
SOUTH INDIAN INSCRIPTIONS
VOLUME XXX
INSCRIPTIONS COLLECTED DURING THE YEAR 1915.

inscription.head:
No. 1.
(A.R. No. 1 of 1915.)
Chidambaram, Chidambaram Taluk, South Arcot District.
Naṭarāja temple-Gōvindarāja perumāḷ shrine-on the north wall of the central shrine.
Achyutadēva-Mahārāya: Śaka 1460, Viḷambi, Paṅguṉi 14,
Monday, Uttara: 3, March 1539 A.D.
inscription.p:
Unfinished. This inscription records the reconsecration of Gōvindarājasvāmin
at Chitrakūṭa and the institution of festivals by the king.

l.gr:
rāmānujāya namaḥ svastiśrī manmahāmaṇdhalēśvara śrīmadacyuta
dēvamahārā

inscription.head:
No. 2.
(A.R. No. 2 of 1915.)
Dārāśuram, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
Irāvatēśvarā Temple-on the north wall of varandah as labels on the
top of the sculptures: 13th century. A.D.
inscription.p: It records the names of devotees who probably served in the temple.

edition.l: īśā[ naśivar]

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1915.)
Kumbhakōnam, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Thanjavur District.
Sōmēśvarasvāmin temple-on a pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the central
shrine
Rājarāja-Chōḻa I: year 5: 990 A.D.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of land by four persons for providing musical instruments to the
god Tirusōmīśvarattu āḻvār at Tirukkuḍamūkkil, a dēvadānam in Pāmbūr-nāḍu. It is laid
down that these musical instruments were to be played on special occasions and the
mūlaparuḍaiyār agreed to carry out this charity.

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1915.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
Sundarēśvara temple-on the east wall of the third gōpura
Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ: year 13, day 306:
13-14th c A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in at the beginning. It records a royal order, granting land as maḍappuṟa
iṟaiyili and tirunandavaṉa-iṟaiyili for the maintenance of a flower garden and also for
feeding two devotees (Māhēśvarās) and two śaiva mendicants (Vairāgyās) daily in the
Nāgaśūriyaṉ maṭha by Mayilērumperumāḷ of Kāttāvūr in Śevvirukkai-nāḍu and
Tatpurusha Śiva alias Paḻi Añjum so ••• •••• The boundaries of the land are
mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(A.R. No. 5 of 1915.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
Sundarēśvara temple-on the south wall of the first gōpura
King name not mentioned: Śaka 1463:
Kīlaka, Friday, pūram: 1541 A.D.
inscription.p:
It records a gift of land for the maṭha situated in Chiṭṭirai street called
Sannathimaṭha presided over by Gñānakūttar alias Sattigñānaterisaṉaikal for performing
māhēśvara-pūja. Reference is made to certain Vaḷḷal maṭha.

l.ta: meṟku tekku ellai mutaliyār vaḷavukku vaṭakku meṟku ellai cittirai
mel nokkina maramum kiḻṉokkiya kiṇaṟum inta maṭattu ••• llum •

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1915.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
Sundarēśvara temple-on the a pillar in the same gōpura
Chokkanātha-Nāyaka: Śaka 1632:
Kara, Paṅguṉi 10, Āyilyam: 1710 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the resettlement of the four villages given to the
sixty-four persons as sarvamāṉya, who were the bearers of the god (image)
Chokkanāthasvāmin earlier. However the arrangement was cancelled later. Unable to
put up with this one person committed suicide by jumping down from the top of the
gōpura of the temple. This convinced the ruler to confirm the grant as sarvamānya.

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1915.)
Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District.
Mīnākshiammaṉ temple-on the east wall of the third prakāra, right of entrance
[Parākrama-Pāṇḍyadēva]: year not mentioned: 14th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
A much damaged inscription contains two verses in praise of a Pāṇḍya king
[Vaḻuti] who had the surname Vāḷāl-vaḻi-tiṟandāṉ. A second set of two verses evidently
in praise of the same king, who is here called Kūḍalūr Kōmāṉ mentions the damming
of the sea with his sword.

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1915.)
Rāmēśvaram, Ramnad Taluk, Ramnad District.
Rāmanātha temple-at the entrance into the central shrine, right side
Sundara Pāṇḍya: year 9+1+1,
Kaṉṉi, 15, Saturday, Magha: 14th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Refers to the daughter (magaḷ) of the deity Uḍaiyār
Tirurāmīśuramuḍaiyanāyanār.

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(A.R. No. 87 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District.
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the central shrine
Vikrama-Chōḻa: year 14: 1287 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of flower-garden (nandāvaṉam) to the temple of
Āḷuḍaiyār Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi Nāyaṉār by Śōḻasiṅgadēvaṉ. The name of the nandāvaṉam
was Ārādakūttaṉ and it was established by the above mentioned individual. Two
persons Vaḷattāvaḻi, the son-in-law of the donor and another person Namaśivāyadēvaṉ
undertook the responsibility of tending the garden. Reference is made to the temple of
Śōḻīśvaramuḍaiya Nāyaṉār, and river Naḷḷāṟu while describing the boundaries.

l.ta: ru[avi]ṭṭa mutalukku makaḷ makaḷ makaḷ kuṭi-

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District.
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the central shrine
King's name and year not mentioned: 14th c A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in. This inscription records a gift of land and a house-site to Kaṟpagachchēri
Kuṇḍūr Piḷḷaṉ alias Sūryabhaṭṭaṉ. The boundaries of the lands and house site are
mentioned. Ulaguyyavaḻu[dāṉ] the pontiff of a maṭha (māḍāpattiyam), figures as one
of the signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muṛuganātha temple-on the south wall of the central shrine
Kōnērinmaikoṇḍāṉ: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of a village in Vīraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu as
t6irumaḍaippaḷḷippuram to the deity of Āḷuḍaiyanāyaṉār of Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi in
Vaḍaparisāra-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the central shrine
Vīkrama-Chōḻa: year 3+1: 1277 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is incomplete and damaged. It records the gift of eight
paḻañjalāgai-achchu by a mendicant of this temple called Piḷḷaiyār Piṟavāneṟikāṭṭiṉāṟ for
providing food offerings and conducting worship to the god on the occasion of the
festival of vasantham, in the month of Vaikāsi etc.

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. 91 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple—on the east and north walls of the same shrine
King name not mentioned: Dhātu, Puraṭṭāsi 5: 14th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription begins with the introduction ‘Chaṇḍēśvaraṉ ōlai.’ It records
the provision of maṉṟāṭṭu right with all appurtenances to a devotee, Āḻvāṉ Aṇṇāmalaiyāṉ
who carried out repairs in the temple. It also states that this maṉṟāṭṭu right is in
addition to the already existing 5 maṉṟāṭṭu which is inforce. A tank by name
Aḻiyāviradaṅkoṇḍāṉ kuḷam is also mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same shrine
King name not mentioned: Īśvara year: 14th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This records the conferment of certain privileges and rights on the three dancing
girls (dēvaraḍiyārs) namely Savuṇḍayanaṅgai, Tiruvuṇṇāḻiyarnaṅgai, and Ālālasundaranaṅgai
of the temple of Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi nāyanār by the temple authorities. It also records
the changes effected in the services rendered to the god ie, in offering worship, reciting
Tiruvembāvai and such other activities.

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the west wall of the maṇḍapa infront of the same shrine
Vīrarājēndra: year 27: 1233 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged and incomplete. It records that a kaikkōḷa, Kāḷi
Avanāsiyāṇ[ḍāṉ] Ellāmvalla Sōḻagaṅgar donated (two) āṉai-achchu sīyakki-paḻañjalāgai
for the livelihood of a person who brings fifty pots of water for the sacred bath of
the deity Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍināyanār. It seems that the temple officials who received
the amount, allotted a plot of land and agreed to remit paddy for the above purpose.

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the same wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīrarājēndra: year 40: 1256 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is built in at the end. It seems to record that Sundaranāḍan, a
devotee (ṭiruvēgambaḍiyār) residing at the temple complex (tirumaḍaiviḷāgam) deposited
certain amount in the temple treasury and the interest accrued out of this amount as
millet was to be utilized for providing food offerings and for the procession of the
deity.

l.ta:
ma tiruvekampaṭiyāril cuntaranāṭanen innāyanār cipaṇṭārattil
oṭukkiṉa ciriyakki paḻañcalākai •••••
paṟum accinvāy kāṇam irutūṇip patakkāka āka kaṇpu

yin piṟṟaināḷ nāyakar tiruvitiyil eḻuntaruḷa itu cantirātittavarai
celvatāka itu panmāhe ••• •••

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the west wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīrarājēndra: year lost: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is badly damaged. Seems to register a gift of land to the
temple by the ascetics of the temple.

l.ta: •••• ṉa tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī virarācentira tevaṟku • ta •••

•••• mārkaḻi māca muta[l] vaṭaparicāra nāṭṭu āḷuṭaiyār
tirumurukanpūṇṭi nā ••••

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. 96 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District.
Muruganātha temple-on the west and south walls of the same maṇḍapa
Vīrarājēndra: year: •• +9: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. It records a gift of oil for cooking and bathing of the god on
Saturdays by Kīran Dēvaṅguḍaiyān Piṟaisūḍuvāṉ Kuṉṟamaṟittaṉ alias Suṭṭiyārumviṭṭa
mūvēndavēḷāṉ, a revenue officer of the temple (Perumāḷ-puravariyār). (see no. 127 of
1915)

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. 96-A of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the west and south walls of the same maṇḍapa
Abhimāṉa-Chōḻa: year 12: 1059 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers some special privileges of honour (variśaigaḷ) conferred
by the king on a brahmaṇa by name Kāśyapaṉ Veṇkāḍaṉ Bavadāsaṉ Nārāyaṇaṉ alias
Abhimāṉasōḻa Bhaṭṭaṉ and the temple servants including dancing girls and drummers
(tapasyar, dēvaraḍiyār and uvachchar) who were residing in the temple complex
(tirumaḍaiviḷāgam). It is stated that they were allowed to have the privilege of sounding
the drums (bhērikai) and gongs (sēkaṇḍi), evidently when they go out in a procession.
They were also given the right to build their house with two floors, two entrances
both in front and back and coating the walls with plaster. They were also entitled to
have this document written on stone and copper plate.

rācanum kiḻeṟṟam kutiraiyu perikai cekaṇṭiyum
koṭṭak

varicaikaḷi-

koḷḷap

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the west and south walls of the same maṇḍapa
Vīkrama-Chōḻa: year 12: 1285 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers a gift of money (śirīyakki-paḻañjalākai-achchu) by
Kappaḍavaṉ Siṟuppuḷiyaṉ alias Pugalivēndaṉ, a merchant of Ayyapoḻil in Vaḍaparisāra-
nāḍu to the temple of Āḷuḍaiyār Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi-uḍaiyār for providing one nāḻi of
cooked rice daily.

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. 98 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the west and south walls of the same maṇḍapa
Vīrarājēndra: year 29+6: 1241 A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. This inscription seems to register some decision taken by various
corporate bodies of merchant community to be followed from the month of Aipasi.
The organizations mentioned are Sāliya-nagarattār, Vāṇiya-maḍigaiyār of Vaḍaparisāra-
naḍu, Akkai-sā[laiyār], Mūttasamakkaṭṭu alias Amaṭṭargaṇḍanterinda-Kaikkōḷar and
Iḷaiyasamakkaṭṭu alias Vīrarājēndra terinda-kaikōḷar of Tañjāvūr alias Maṇṇiyūr-aḍikkīṭṭaḷam.

l.ta:
kovirācakecaripaṟparāna tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ
cirivirarācentira tevaṟku yāṇṭu irupattonpatāvatuk ketir
āṟāvatu aṟpicaittiṅkaḷ mutalāka vaṭaparicāra nāṭṭil
cāliyanakarattomum vāṇiyamaṭikaiyo ••••

yomum akkaicā[laiyo]mum mettalait tañcā[vū]rāṉa manniyūr
aṭikkiṭṭaḷattil muttacamakkaṭṭāṉa amaṭṭamar kaṇṭan terinta
kaikkoḷaromum cāmantar cenāpatikaḷomum iḷaiya camakkaṭṭāna
virarācentiran terinta kaikkoḷaro ••• ••••

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the west and south walls of the same maṇḍapa
Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān: year 15+1: 1223 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records gift of the village Niyamam alias Vīrarājēndra-nallūr on
the south Bank in Vaḍaparisāra-nāḍu for providing food offerings, worship, and
repairs, to the shrine of Tirukkāmakkōṭṭa-nāchchiyār named Irugunuṇṇiḍai maṅgaiyār
of this temple for the merit of Āṇḍakkaṉ. All the taxes (iṟaipuravu) such as
chiṟṟāyam, elavai, ukavai, maṉṟupāḍu teṇḍakuṟṟam, vēṇḍukōḷ, eruttiṟai, sumai-suṅgam,
sārikai and others were granted to the temple. Ārutakottikkōyilāṉ Toṇḍaṉ Nampiḷḷāṉ
alias Brahmarāyaṉ, Nakkaṉdēvaṉ, Nakkaṉpotti, Siṟunakkaṉpotti, Tonṟi-tonṟi, all belonging
to Ātrēyagōtra were given the right of one maṉrāṭṭu, while Periyacheṭṭi-piḷḷāṉ, Cheṭṭi-
Kēsaṉ, Cheṭṭi-Siṟukēsaṉ all belonging to Kāśyapagōtra were collectively given one
maṉrāṭṭu, and Kēsan Kaṇṇaṉ, Kaṇṇaṉ Pāṇḍavataraiyaṉ, Kōvaṉ-Kiḷḷai all belonging to
Pūluva Vēṭṭuva were given the right of ūrāḷmai. It is also stated that the above persons
had been invested with the responsibility of looking after the settled people and the
right of colonizing the village with new families into Vaḍaparisāra-nāḍu. It is stipulated
that Poṉṉaṉ-piḷḷāṉ, alias Īśvarabhaṭṭa of Kāśyapagōtra, the priest of the temple is
responsible for carrying out the repair works. Ellāmvalla Sōḻa-mūvēndavēḷaṉ,
Tiyāgavinōdhaṉ, Kaḷappāḷarāyaṉ and others figure as signatories.

tiripuvanaccakkaravatti koneri[ṉ]maikoṇṭān āḷuṭaiyār
tirumurukanpūṇṭi nāyanāṟku āṇṭakkanukku nanṟāka tirukkāmakkoṭṭa

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 100 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīrarājēndra: year 29+5: 1240 A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. This inscription seems to record a gift by Kāmaṉ Siṟupiḷḷai alias
Uttama ••• ••••• , a kaikkōḷa residing at the temple complex (tirumaḍaiviḷāgam) of the
temple.

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 101 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kōnērinmaikoṇḍāṉ: year 5: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This records the renumeration of 48 kalam of paddy by the king to the
devotee, Palaviḷakkap-pichchar for tending the garden Vīrasōḻaṉ Tirunandāvaṉam. It
is also stated that he was to get the above 48 kalam from his share of taxes collected
from Palloḍam alias Adirādhirāśanallūr. Sembiyan Uttaramantri figures as a signatory.

l.ta: ṭu palloṭammā[na] atirātirācanallūr nam iṟaik kaṇ-
ka namōlai kuṭuttom ivai cempiyan uttaraman-

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 102 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḻa: year 3: 1152 A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription registers a gift of paddy for feeding mendicants
(tapasvins) probably by Agōrasivaṉ from the lands irrigated by the big dam (periya
aṇai). The quantity of rice to be given each day to each person is specified and
provision was also made for the years containing an extra month (adhika-māsa).

tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīku[lottuṅka c]oḻa tevaṟku
yāṇṭu muṉṟāvatu āṉittiṅkaḷ ••• ••••• l viya •••••

akoracivaṉeṉ itu pan

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. 103 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīra-Chōḻa: year 18: 1186 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is incomplete and damaged. It seems to record the gift of land
for providing food offerings to Kshētrapālap-piḷḷaiyār of this temple by some persons
who were described as Piḷḷaippēruḍaiyārgaḷ (literally, those who had the surname Pillai)
of Rāsarāsapuram. The boundaries of the endowed land are mentioned.

l.ta: ku yāṇṭu patineṭṭāvatu •••• ytū nāṭṭu i-
ku teṟkkum araṅkameṭṭukku pā[y*]ki[ṟa ka]varukku ki-
ḻakkum ūrāḷi uḻukiṟa kavarukku vaṭakkum innā •••

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. 104 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kōṉērinmaikoṇḍāṉ: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. This inscription records the order of the king to the temple officials
(Dēvakaṉmis) of Muruganātha temple for providing food offerings.

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. 105 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha Temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kōṉērinmaikoṇḍāṉ: year lost: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription records the apportionment of taxes between the
temples of Avināsi-āṇḍār and Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi-mahādēvar. It also records that ten
achchu had been deposited for providing food offerings to the god at Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. 106 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Uttama-Chōḻa: 12th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in and damaged. It seems that this record registers a gift for offering made
by the village administrative people (ūrār and ūrāḷikaḷ) of Ottanūr.

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. 107 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḻa: year 13: 1162 A.D.
inscription.p:
It records a gift of money (paḻañjalāgai-achchu) for a twilight lamp by Kāḍaṉ Āḷuḍaiyāṉ,
one of the Tirunīlakaṇṭamuḍaiyārs and a native of Paḍakkēpaḷḷi, in Vaḍapariśāra-nāḍu.

kolottuṅkacoḻa teva[r*]kku tiruve ••• ṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 108 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīra-Pāṇḍya: year 3: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Stones are out of order. Below this is an incomplete inscription and disjointed
pieces. It records the gift of land after purchase for providing food offering to the god
Ugarayāṇḍār by Siṟiyāṉ alias Śivaṉukkiniyāṉ and Āṇḍāṉ Ālālas[undaraṉ] by depositing
24 coins at temple treasury.

l.ta: •• ṇpān ciṟiyān civanukkiniyānum āṇṭāṉ ālāla cu-

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(A.R. No. 109 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
King name lost: Year: [4]: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This is much damaged. It seems to record the gift of 4 [paḻañja]lākai-achchu to
Ugarayānḍār, by a merchant called Śiṟiyān Āṇḍā[ṉ].

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(A.R. No. 110 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha Temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīkrama-Chōḻa: Year 3: 1276 A.D.
inscription.p:
It records the decision taken by Chitramēḻi-periyanāṭṭār of Vaḍaparisāra-nāḍu
and the nāṭṭār of Pūluva-nāḍu. According by the families which remit elavai must
supply one kuṟuṇi, and the farmers of Pūluva-nāḍu were to supply four nāḻi of millet
to the temple of Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi-nāyanār.

l.ta:
vikkiramacoḻatevaṟku yā[ṇ]ṭu [muṉ]ṟā[va*]tu vaṭaparicāra
nāṭṭil cittirameḻip periyanā[ṭ*]ṭomum pūluva nāṭṭomum

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha Temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīrarājēndra: year 21+3: 1231 A.D.
inscription.p:
Records the deposit of one paḻañsalākai-achchu by Umaiyāḷ Maiviravukaṇṇi, a
dancing girl (dēvaraḍiyāḷ) of Koḻumam for a twilight lamp to the temple of
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi Āḷuḍaiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 35.
(A.R. No. 112 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Uttama-Chōḷa: ••• : 12th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is much damaged. It records the gift of land for food
offering to Tirūmurugaṉpūṇḍi Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. 113 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kōnērinmaikoṇḍān : ••• : 12th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the setting up of an image of Goddess for bed-chamber
(Paḷḷiarai nāchchiyār) by the order of the king and also gift of land for providing food
offering to the goddess. The quantity of rice and other dishes to be offered are also
specified.

l.ta: m canti[rātit*]tavarai celvitāka nammolai kuṭuttom cem-

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. 114 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ: year 20: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the royal order of making gift of paddy for providing
food offerings and worship (pūjāvritti) to Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi Āṇdār. It is also stated
that the paddy donated was from the remittence of the taxes by the sabhaiyār of
Amarabhuyaṅgara-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and the ūrār of Veḷḷaṉūr to the king.

kalamum āka ne-

caruppetima-

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(A.R. No. 115 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kōneriṉmaikoṇḍān: ••• : 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged and last few lines are missing. It records the royal
order sent to the assembly of Nallūr alias Amarabhuyaṅkara-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Vīrachōḻa vaḷa-nāḍu and the ūrār of Veḷḷānūr, of giving particular amount of paddy to
the person as pūjāvritti, who was doing worship in the temple of Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi.

koṇṭāṉ viracoḻa vaḷanāṭṭu nallūrāṉa amarapuyaṅkarac caruppeti

m cilaiyilum veṭṭik koḷvutāka nammolai ••• ṇikka mu • •

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(A.R. No. 116 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḻa: year 9: 1158 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged and built in at the end. It records the deposit of
20 kaḻañju of gold for providing food offerings to the god Kūttāḍumdēvar [Naṭarāja].
The measure Parakēsari-nāḻi is mentioned.

iru na ••• ••••

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(A.R. No. 117 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīrarājēndra: year 27: 1233 A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in. It records that Avināsi alais Ellāmvalla Sōḻakkar made a gift of three
āṉai achchu Srīyakki-paḻañsalāgai achchu and three paṇam for the persons bringing 50
pots of water daily for bathing the god. Three persons who received the gift agreed
to provide the same out of the interest of the endowed amount.

mātam mutal vaṭapari •••

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(A.R. No. 118 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple—on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīrarājēndra: year 29+7: 1242 A.D.
inscription.p:
It is damaged and incomplete. It records the endowment made by Āṇḍāṉ
Vāṇarāyadēva, minister (sāmantar) for providing the sacred bath and food offering to
the deity on Śivarātri days.

l.ta:
tāvatukketir eḻāvatu ••• tai māta mutal
perumāḷ

cāmantaril āṇṭāṉ vāṇarāyatevane ••• pūṇṭi nāyaṉāṟku
civarāttiraikku canti nālukku amutupaṭi celavu kālālarici kalaṉe

••• ••• ••••

inscription.head:
No. 42.
(A.R. No. 119 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīkrama-Chōḻa: year 13 : 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of 12 sirīyakki-paḻañjalāgai achchu for feeding
of āṇḍārs (devotees) by a certain Mutti Āṇḍi Ammai, wife of a merchant named Piḷḷān
of Karaiyānaḍikkīḻtaḷam in Vaḍaparisāra-nāḍu. One nāḻi rice per achchu was to be
supplied for this purpose.

l.ta:
m vaṭaparicāra nāṭṭil kaṟaiyānaṭik kiḻttaḷattil viyāpāri piḷḷāṉ
[maṉaiyāṭṭi]
••• ṭayān kūttan eḻuttu ippaṭikku maṇalūruṭaiyān paḷa-
ñciran eḻuttu ippaṭikku ••• ṟuruṭaiyān āḻvān eḻuttu ||-

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(A.R. No. 120 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīrarājēndra: year 29+1: 1237 A.D.
inscription.p:
Records that a lady Amaṭṭan Sivaṇaimuḻutuḍaiyāḷ gifted four śrī paḻañjalāgai
achchu for providing various food offerings (piṭṭu, paṇṇiyāram, appam) on Sundays to
the god Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi-nāyanār.

l.ta:
rāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ virarācentira tevaṟku
tirunalliyāṇṭu irupa-

ttonpatāvati netirāvatu cittirai mātam āḷuṭaiyār tirumurukanpūṇṭi
nāyanāṟku tirunāyiṟṟukkiḻa-

mai piṭṭamutukku tiruppaṇṇiyārāppattukku arici nānāḻiyum teṅkāy

panmākecu

inscription.head:
No. 44.
(A.R. No. 121 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vikrama-Chōḻa: year 4: 1277 A.D.
inscription.p:
It records gift of one achchu by one Jayasiṅgadēvaṉ perumān alias
Aḻagiyapāṇḍyadēvaṉ for burning a twilight lamp to the god at Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi. The
Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple received the gift and agreed to burn the lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(A.R. No. 122 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kōnērinmaikoṇḍāṉ: year 3: 12th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. It contains the royal order addressed to two Śivabrāhmaṇās of
Āḷuḍaiyār temple at Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi. It appears that during the rule of his younger
brother certain Murugāṇḍāṉ deposited some amount and tried to make the kāṇi right
of the two priests null and void. The aggrieved persons appealed to the king who
restored their right after they deposited fifteen achchu.

l.ta: ḷai nāyanāṉa ponṉampala nampikkum po[n*]naṉ pukaliventa-
nukkum nam ōlai kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu yināyanā[r*] koyil
tara namakku munṟāvatu nāḷile nampakkale ••• eṅkā-
ṇi eṅkaḷukku ākavaraṇumenṟu namakku tarum accu pat-
tum toṇṭaimāṉ peṟuvari muṟaimaikku accu añcum [āka]

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(A.R. No. 123 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīrarājēndra: year 44 : 1251 A.D.
inscription.p:
Records that the Śivabrāhmaṇas Avināsi Kāḻiyaṉ and three others received 24
āṉaiachchu Śriyakki-paḻañjalāgai from the treasury of the temple at Tirumuruganpūṇḍi
and agreed to burn four perpetual lamps at the temple.

l.ta:
vatu cittirait
tiṅkaḷ vaṭaparicāra nāṭṭil āḷuṭai-
yār tirumurukanpūṇṭi nāyanār koyilik kāṇiyuṭaiya civa

ci kāḻiyānum avināci cāntāraikkāppānum eṅkaḷ makkaḷ aviṉā[ciavi]ṉāciyum
aviṉācimuruka[ṉu]-

itu cantirātittavarai celvatākavum cempilum cilaiyi-

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. 124 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha Temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kōnērinmaikoṇḍāṉ: ••• : 12th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged and incomplete. It seems to refer to the setting up
of an image of the goddess in the temple of Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi Āḷuḍaiyār for the merit
of Āṇḍakkaṉ Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻi-Āḻviyār.

l.ta:
tirupuvaṉaccakkaravatti konerinmai koṇṭān āḷuṭaiyār
tirumurukan pūṇṭi āḷuṭaiyāṟku āṇṭakkan ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻi
āḻviyāṟku nanṟāka tirukkāmak ••• ••• •••• [r*]

eḻuntaruḷu
vippatāka innācciyār koyiṟ tevarkanmikaḷukku nam ••• •••••

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(A.R. No. 125 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Vīkrama-Chōḻa: year 11: 1284 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of money (one paḻañjalāgai) for burning a
twilight lamp in the Āḷuḍaiyār temple by a brāhmaṇa named Saivapurandara-chakravartiṉ
who had the kāṇi right in the temple of Āḷuḍaiyār Vallāḷagaṇḍar at Īṅgaiyūr in
Pūnduṟai-nāḍu.

l.ta:
vatu pūntuṟai nāṭṭu
yiṅkaiyūr āḷuṭaiyār vallāḷa kaṇṭar koyil kāṇiyuṭaiya
civa

vva purantarac cakkaravattiyen vaṭaparicāra nāṭṭil āḷuṭaiyā[r*]
tirumurukanpūṇṭi nāyanāṟku nān vaitta cantiyātipam onṟukku oṭukkiṉa
paḻañcalākai accu onṟu yitil upaiyaṅkoṇṭu cantirātittavarai
celvatāka

inscription.head:
No. 49.
(A.R. No. 126 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple—on a pillar of the same maṇḍapa
Vīkrama-Chōḻa: year 14: 1287 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged at the end. It records a gift of money (paḻañjalāgai)
by Aḻagiyanāchchi āḻvi one of the ladies (peṇḍugaḷ) of the palace establishment (vēḷam).
The money was deposited in the temple treasury of Tirumurugaṉ pūṇḍi Āḷuḍaiya-
nāyaṉār.

inscription.head:
No. 50.
(A.R. No. 127 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha Temple—on another pillar in the same place
Vīrarājēndra: Year 29+5: 1241 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of this pillar by Kīraṇ Dēvaṅguḍaiyān Piṟai
Śūḍuvān Kuṇṟameṟindāṉ alias Suṭṭiyāḷaviṭṭa Chōḻamūvēndavēḷāṉ, who was one of the
revenue officers of the king (Perumāḷ puravariyār).

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha Temple—on another pillar in the same maṇḍapa
Vikrama-Chōḻa: year 3: 13th A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records gift of gold (poṉ) for burning a twilight lamp by a
merchant named Kūttaṉ Dēvaṉ alias Dēvāramaḻagiyār, a resident of Śēvūr in Vaḍaparisāra-
nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 129 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha Temple—on another pillar in the same maṇḍapa
Vīrarājēndra: year 29+3: 1238 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records that the gift of this pillar by Karuñchemban Nambi, a
merchant of Karaiyāṉaḍikkīṭṭaḷam.

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha Temple-on the south wall of the
enclosure round the same maṇḍapa, inner side
Jat. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 25: 1310 A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Records the meeting of the tāṉattār of Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi Āḷuḍaiya-
nāyaṉār and the members of Aḍikkiḻttaḷam at Aṇuvaṉ toṇḍaṉ Tirukkāvāṇam. They
agreed to donate a piece of land to the temple and agreed to engraved at Perumpaḻaṉaṭṭār
uḍaiyār temple.

l.ta: murukaṉpūṇṭi āḷuṭaiya ṉāyaṉār koyil tāṉa[tto*]mum a-
r uṭaiyār tirumalaiyil kalveṭṭiṉapa[ṭi] na[ma]tu uḻukaiyil
cūriyatevar maṉiya tevar yivvellaikku cettuṅ ke-
ti •••• varkku yiṟaiyāka viṭāṟu melaivayalil ca •••

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha Temple-on the south and east walls of the
Goddess shrine in the same temple.
14th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription praises the act of making and protecting the
gifts. It records the donation of money to the temple by a dancing girl namely
Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira Naṅgai, which was entrusted with the temple trustees and mendicants
(kōvaṇavar, tāṉattār and virabhatṟas) of the temple. It also records some privileges
given to her while worshipping the deity, such as showing flywhisk, mirror and singing
songs with gesture etc.,

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Muruganātha temple—on the north wall of the same shrine
Jat. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 4: 1289 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the pavement of floor in the ardha-maṇḍapa of
the goddess shrine (Irugunuṇṇiḍai-maṅgaiyār) in the temple of Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi
Āluḍaiya Nāyaṉār by Mayilēṟuvāṉ Vaḷattāṉ alias Tiruppaṇi Kāṅgēyaṉ, a native of
Āḍippuliyūr in Chōḻamaṇḍalam.

l.ta:
uṭaiyāṉ mayileṟuvāṉ vaḷattāṉāṉa tiruppaṇik kāṅkeyaneṉ
uṭaiyār tirumurukaṉpūṇṭiyāḷuṭaiya nāyaṉār nācciyā-

r irukunuṇṇiṭaimaṅkaiyār koyil a[r*]ttamaṇṭapam
taḷamicaippitteṉ itupaṉmā

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Mādavīśvara temple—on a pillar of the maṇḍapa infront of the shrine
inscription.p:
Written in modern characters and incomplete. It registers the building activities
carried out at the front hall (mahāmaṇḍapa) providing tiles to its roof and renewing
of the walls in the temple of Mādavi-Īsvarar by Dāyar Koṭṭagaṉ son of Paḻaṉiyappa
Koṭṭagaṉ, a vosalthy blacksmith, residing in Nilakkōṭṭai.

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1915.)
Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District
Kūppidupiḷḷaiyār temple-on the north wall of the shrine on a rock near the same village
Vīkrama-Chōḻa: year 12: 1285 A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. This inscription records a gift of money (4 paḻañjalāgai achchu)
probably for offering sandal paste by one of the saints namely Namaśivāyar who
deposited the amount with the priests (Śivabrāhamanas) of the temple named Sīgāḻi and
Avināsimurugaṉ.

l.ta:
murukan uḷḷiṭṭomum c(ā)nta[ṇa] kāppu^1^ ••• num meṟpaṭi nāyanāraik
kumpiṭṭi

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1915.)
Guḍimaṅgalam, Udumalpet Taluk, Coimbatore District
Chōḻīsvarā Temple-on the south wall
Kōnēriṉmaikoṇdāṉ: year 15: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This is a fragmentary inscription which seems to be a record of grant of certain
privileges to the Kammalas [artisans] of South Koṅgu (teṉ Koṅgu) including making
use of mortar for their houses.

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1915.)
Guḍimaṅgalam, Udumalpet Taluk, Coimbatore District
On a stone set up in a field near the same village.
Achyutarāya Mahārāya: Śakā 1458, Durmukhi, Arpaśi, 27, Dvādaśi, Wednesday,
Uttara: 1536 A.D. Wed. Oct. 11
inscription.p:
Records the gift of the village Pūlaippāḍikkālpaḷḷi alias Periyamaṅgalam in South
Poṅgalūṟkā-nāḍu to a brāhmaṇa choultry of that place, by the Mahāmaṇḍalīśvara
Vālaiyadēvamahārāsar, for the merit of the Vijayanagara king Vīrapratāpa Vīra Acyutarāya
Mahārāya. Among the boundaries a piece of land owned by Nāchchiyappa Nayiṉār's
is mentioned. This record also states that the brāhmaṇa who was supervising the
choultry was also entitled to enjoy the property.

l.ta: vāramum uttira nakṣa[tti*]ramum peṟṟa ṉāḷ śrī-
rācar nammuṭaiya cuvāmi accu(t)tarāya makārāyaṟkku pu-

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 137 of 1915.)
Guḍimaṅgalam, Udumalpet Taluk, Coimbatore District
On a slab lying near a bridge in the same village
Dundubhi, Kārttikai, Thursday, pañchami, uttarāshaḍha, 15:
Modern characters
inscription.p:
Inscription A records that one Malaiya Nāyakkar of Maṅgalam village
donated a land as bhūdhāṉam to a brāhmaṇa on the occasion of his marriage.
Inscription B also refers the similar gift and C is much damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. 138 of 1915.)
Śittaḷundūr, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Śīrgāḻinātha temple-on a slab set up in the courtyard
Krishṇadēva Mahārāya: Śaka 1455 (mistaken for 1445), Svabhānu, Kārttika 23,
paurnima, Sunday,: 1523 A.D. Sunday, Nov. 22
inscription.p:
Last portion is badly damaged. Records the gift of land at Śiṟṟaḷundūr in
Tirumalai Kāṅgēyaṉ sīrmai alias Śiṟṟaḷundūr of Mōrūr, situated in Ēḻugarai Pūnduṟai
nāḍu by one Sāma Nāyaṉār, an agent of Triyambaka uḍaiyār who was ruling over
Muḷvāy Eyinūrchchāvaḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1915.)
Śittaḻundūr, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Attanūrammaṉ temple-on the four pillars of the maṇḍapa in front of the shrine
Dundubhi, A4ṉi, 22, Friday: Modern characters.
inscription.p:
Records that these pillars were gifted by four individuals namely Nallamakavuṇḍaṉ
belonging to Puḷiyampaṭṭi, Olagappa Kavuṇḍaṉ, grandson of Komāṟasāmik Kavuṇḍaṉ
of Olagappampāḷaiyam, Rāmasāmi Kavuṇḍaṉ, grandson of Nallaya Kavuṇḍaṉ of
Devaḍipāḷaiyam, Tulukavuṇḍaṉ, grandson of Nallaya Kavuṇḍaṉ of Sāmappāḷaiyam.

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1915.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
On a slab set up in the Kaikkōḷa street
Krishṇadēva-Mahārāya: Chitrabhānu, Āvaṇi 20: 14th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. This inscription records the gift of all taxes such as irai-mānya,
śuṅga, and maṉaivari, to be collected from the settlers, peasants and merchants of
Sāmasamudram, a village founded by one Sāma Nayiṉār in his own name. The latter
was the son of Māṇikka Nayiṉār of Kāñchīpuram, which is said to have been located
on the northern bank of the river Pālāṟu in the Chandragirich-chāvaḍi in Toṇḍai-
maṇḍalam, and might have been under his control. Sāma Nayiṉār is mentioned in this
record as an agent of Triyampaka Uḍaiyār, son of Tipparāsa Uḍaiyār who governed
the Muḷvāy area during the period of the Vijayanagara king Vīrapratāpa Krishṇarāya
Mahārāya. In this record Tiruchcheṅgōḍu is mentioned as the Tiruppaḍaivīḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1915.)
Tiruchcheṅgōḍu Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
On another slab set up near the tank in the same village
Viya, Vaikāsi, 24: Modern characters.
inscription.p:
Records the erection of a platform (sumai tāṅgi), in memory of one Lachchumāyi,
who was the daughter of Kavalamuttu son of Kēthāralinga mudali, by her mother
Paḻaniammā. Lachchumāyi's husband's name was Aruṇāchala mudali who was the son
of Koḻundavēl mudali, a native of Kavuṇḍaṉpāḷaiyam village.

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 142 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Bhāṇīśvara temple-on the west wall of the maṇḍapa
Infront of the central shrine.
Nandana, Māśi, 19: Modern characters.
inscription.p:
Registers the dēvadāṉa gift of a tank with the ratio of 2 shares to Vāṉīśvarā and
one to Ādinārāyaṇapperumāḷ by Avināśikkavuṇḍaṉ for the merit of Koṉappayaṉ,
Gōpālayaṉ, officials under Tirumalai Nāyaka. Vīrarāgava mudaliār, Chokkanāta mudaliār,
Nāyanāta mudaliār also seem to be the officials of that region. Nātiyappa, an
accountant of the sabha of nāḍu is mentioned as signatory.

nāḷ tirumalaiṉāyakkara[y*]yaṉ kāriyattukku kaṟttarāṉa

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 143 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Bhāṇīśvara temple-on the same wall
Narasiṅgarāya: Śaka 1420 Kāḷayukti, Chittirai,
Sunday, Hasta: 1498 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of a village called Suviratapuram as a
dēvadāṉa to the temple of Bhānappīśvara Nayiṉār at Parittippaḷḷi-nāḍu in Kuṉṟattūr-
paṟṟu surrounding Muḷvāy-in Koṅgu alias Vīraśōḻa-maṇḍalam by Viṭṭamarasar, an
agent, for the merit of Narasaṇa Nāyakka Uḍaiyār.

mum peṟṟa nāyaṟṟukiḻamai
nāḷ koṅkāṉa vīra-
coḻamaṇṭalattu muḷvāy cūḻnta kuṉṟattūrp paṟṟil pari

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Bhāṇīśvara temple-on a pillar in the same maṇḍapa
[Sundara]-Pāṇḍya: year 24: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of this pillar (bearing the inscription) by Tillaiyaḻaganambi of
Kātyāyaṇa gōtrā a native of Paruttippaḷḷi alias Rājasiṁha-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Bhāṇīśvara temple-on another pillar in the same maṇḍapa
in front of the central shrine.
Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 24: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
It records the gift of this pillar, its cornice and the beam by a certain Periya
piḷḷaiyāṇḍai Karumpiḷḷai, a native of Miṉṉāmoḻi in Paruttippaḷḷi-nāḍu.

edition.l: melpākkal 4-

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Bhāṇīśvara temple-on another pillar in the same maṇḍapa
Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 24: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of this pillar by Kaṇṇuvāṉ Appaṉ, a black-smith and a native
of Paruttippaḷḷi.

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Bhāṇīśvara temple-on another pillar in the same maṇḍapa
Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 24: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
States that the gift of this pillar made by Nāyan Tiruchchiaṟṟmbala nambi
Uttamanambi of kauśika gōtra also a native of Perumarudūr and one among an
aṟamūppar of Paruttippaḷḷi alias Rājasimha-chaturvēdi-maṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Bhāṇīśvara temple-on the west wall of the Sīrgāḻinātha shrine
Vīrarājēndra-Chōḻa: year 23: 1229 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the renovation of this stone temple (tirukkaṟṟaḷi) by
Adiyamāṉ-naḍuvilnaṅgaṉ, one among the Pāṇḍi vēṭṭuvar an administrator of the village
(ūrāḷi) Paruttippaḷḷi in Nāḍāḻvār-nāḍu.

l.ta: vatu nāṭāḻvār nāṭṭup paruttippaḷḷi teva[r*] k[o]-

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Bhāṇīśvara temple-on the south wall of the same shrine
Rājēndra-Chōḻa: year lost: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged at the right end. It records a gift of land most probably to the temple
of Sri Kailāsamuḍaiyanāyaṉār at Paruttippaḷḷi in Nāḍāḻvāṉ-nāḍu by certain individuals
to conduct festival for their welfare. The donated land was located on the eastern side
of the big tank.

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. 150 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Ādikēśava perumāḷ temple-on the south wall of the central shrine
Vīra-Rāmanātha: year 19: 1274 A.D.
inscription.p:
It records a gift of door jamb (tirunilaikkāl) of the ardha-maṇḍapa of the
temple of Chittiramēḻi-viṇṇagar alias Ādikēśavap-perumāḷ at Paruttippaḷḷi alias Rājasiṁhach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, in Śēla-nāḍu, by Kañjamalai-dēvaṉ a Mudali of Karambās of
Paruttippaḷḷi.

l.ta:
c cittirame[ḻi vi]ṇṇakarāna
ātikecavap perumāḷ tirukkoyil tiru a-

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Ādikēsava perumāḷ temple-on the south wall
14th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
It records a gift of conch, bell, bērigai (gong), trumpet (sēgaṇḍigai) by Mātayāma
Tattān Poṉṉāṇḍai, a merchant of Paruttippaḷḷi who is also described as a native of
Vālikaṇḍapuram.

l.ta: yāma tattān poṉṉāṇṭai yiṭṭa tanmam ūtu caṅ-
ku yonṟum tūpamaṇi yonṟum perikai oṉṟum cekaṇ-

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District.
Ādikēsava perumāḷ temple-on the south wall of the same shrine
Vīra-Rāmanātha: year 19: 1274 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a donation of two door jambs (tirunilaikkāl) to the
central shrine of the Chittiramēḻi-viṇṇagar alias Ādikēsavap-perumāḷ temple at Paruttippaḷḷi
alias Rājasiṅgach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam by Silambāṇḍai, son of Kaṇṇandai Siṟiyāṇḍai, of
Kachchippaḷḷi for the merit of his uncle (māmaṉ) Sempōttar Piḷḷaiyāṇḍai.

ttuc cittirameḻiviṇṇakarāṉa ātikecavapperumāḷ

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. 153 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District.
Ādikēsava perumāḷ temple-on the east wall of the maṇḍapa
Jat. Vīra-Pāṇḍya: 15+5 year: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Records a gift of land (42 kuḻi) to the temple of Ādikēsavap-perumāḷ
temple in Rājasiṅgach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam by a blacksmith saṇṇa •• one of the
Mudalis of Paruttippaḷḷi.

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. 154 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District.
Aḻaganāchchiyammaṉ temple-on the west, east and south walls.
Dēvarāya: 15th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription seems to be a record of the construction of this
temple from the foundation to the eaves (vyaḷa) i.e. Adhittana to Kapotha vyalavari
[row of lion like animal] [except griva & sigara] by a Śivabhaktha Attānikkāmiṇḍar
who was also a Veḷḷaḷa.

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. 155 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District.
Aḻaganāchchiyammaṉ temple-on the same walls.
Vijaya-Bukkarāya: Sarvadhāri, Ani, 3: 15th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription seems to record the construction of the temple by
the blacksmiths including Bhūmikkāmiṇdar and Mudalikkāmiṇḍar who had the kāni
right in the village.

uḷḷiṭṭa kāṇiyāḷarom eṅkaḷopātikku jana

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. 156 of 1915.)
Paruttippaḷḷi, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District.
On a slab set up in a field near the same village
Jat. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: year 14: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of the entire village Adirāpuram as sarvamānya for
conducting festival in the month of Māsi at the temple of Vēdanāyaka-Perumāḷ in
Tirunārāyaṇapuram in Karai-nāḍu by the nāṭṭavar [assembly members] of Pūvāṇiya-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. 157 of 1915.)
Maṅgalam, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
On a slab set up near the Aḻaganāchchiyamman temple
Vīra Rājendra (Kulōttuṅga)-Chōḻa III: year 8: [1126 A.D.]
inscription.p:
Records that the body Munnūṟṟuvar of Nālukarai-nāḍu had agreed to protect
Maṇgalam, a dēvadāna village of Rājasēkara-Īsvaramuḍaiya-Mahādēva temple built by
Koṅgilāṇḍār a resident of Mudalināyappaḷḷi alias Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻapuram. This record was
written by Bhaṭṭālagan Vīrakēsari alias Irumuḍi Iḍaṅgai uyyakkoṇḍān of Siṟuvūr in
Kiḷiyūr nāḍu.

edition.l: ••• ntra [ coḻadēvaṟku yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 158 of 1915.)
Mallaśamudram, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Chōḻēsvara temple-on the south wall of the central shrine
Modern characters
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. It seems to record some gift by Sāmi[p]iḷḷai sambaṉ for food
offerings and three twilight lamps. The village names of Paruttippaḷḷi and Mallaisamuttiram
are mentioned.

l.ta: taṉma naṭakkum pūcaikkaṭ[ṭa*]ḷai piṉ pittaikku mūṉṟu-
m orupaṭi ney kālpaṭi kaṟiy muṉṟu cantiyātipam • •

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. 159 of 1915.)
Mallasamudram, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Chōḻēśvara temple-on Detached stones built into the east,
North and south walls of the same shrine and the maṇḍapa in front of it.
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: . 8: 11th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Stones are not in order. It begins with the eulogy of Rājēndra I.
It is in many pieces built into the wall. probably belonged to one and the same
temple, Ulagaḷandāṉ Viṇṇagar of Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḷapuram. It appears a gift was made
for the food offerings to the deity (Āḷvār) of Ulagaḷandāṉ viṇṇagar by the nagarattār
of Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻapuram.

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. 160 of 1915.)
Mallaśamudram, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Chōḻēśvara temple-on the Nandi maṇḍapa in front of the same shrine
Bahudhānya, Tai.: Modern characters
inscription.p:
It records that this nandi-maṇḍapa was constructed by certain Krishṇa seṭṭi, son
of Kōṉēri seṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. 161 of 1915.)
Mallaśamudram, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
Chōḻēśvara temple-on the Dhvajastambha of the same temple
Kāḷayukta, Āvaṇi.: Modern characters
inscription.p:
Records that this dhvajastambha was gifted by certain Kōṉēri seṭṭi, son of
Tirumalai seṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. 162 of 1915.)
Toṭṭipālaiyam, Tiruchchengodu Taluk, Salem District
On the rock called Pondakkal Perumāḷmalai, near the village
Raudri, Puraṭṭāsi, 21.: Modern characters
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift by certain Muttukkavuṇḍaṉ, son of Kuḷantakkavuṇḍaṉ
of P[oṅgu]richcha Dēvaṉpāḷaiyam.

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. 163 of 1915.)
Kiḻputtūr, Wandiwash Taluk, North Arcot District
Svayambhunāthasvāmi temple-on the north and west walls of the central shrine
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḻa: year 6: 12th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Unfinished. This inscription records a gift of land (2050 kuḻi,) by purchase to
the temple of Tirukkumārīsvaramuḍaiyār at Irukkalāmputtūr in Poṉṉūr-nāḍu, a sub
division of Veṇkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam, a sub-division of Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam by certain
Nālāyiravaṉ Sīvālaya santōḻaṉ Seṅkēṇi Ammaiyappaṉ, for the midnight offerings. This
gifted land includes 1000 kuḻi of land at Aḍaichcheṟuvu and Pūvaṅgaḻuval, 120 kuḻi of
land at Maṇikuṇḍu, 250 kuḻi of land at Pullānti, 450 kuḻi of land at Perumaṇali, 180
kuḻi of land at Nīr Nāval Kuṇḍu and Aḻiñjil Kuṇḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(A.R. No. 164 of 1915.)
Kīḻputtūr, Wandiwāsh Taluk, North Arcot District
Svayambhunāthasvāmi temple-on the south, east and north walls of the
same shrine and the maṇḍapa in front of it
Vikrama-Chōḻa: year 5: 1123 A.D.
inscription.p:
This record which engraved in the fifth regnal year of the Chōḻa king Vikrama
Chōḻadēva, is a copy of an earlier inscription dating back to the fourth regnal year of Vijayālayadēva.
This document registers the detailed list of lands granted as dēvadāna to the temple of Āḷuḍaiyār
Tirukkumarīsvaramuḍaiyār at Irukkalāmputtūr in Poṉṉur-nāḍu, a subdivision of
Veṇkuṉrak-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam with their names as they are locally
known and their proper measurements as follows:
1. Viḷakkupaṭṭi-500 kuḻi
2. Umainaṅgai-350 kuḻi
3. Nankuṟai-320 kuḻi
4. Māñcheṟu-300 kuḻi
5. Thaḻuvakkuḻaiñchāṉ-120 kuḻi
6. Tirumañjaṇakuṇḍu-100 kuḻi
7. Poṉpaḍukuṭṭam-240 kuḻi
8. Tirichchiṟṟambala-peruñjeṟu-300 kuḻi
9. Koḍaiyaṉcheṟu-400 kuḻi
10. Gaṇavathi kuṇḍu-40 kuḻi
11. Pūvilaik kuṇḍu-230 kuḻi
12. Chaṇḍēsvaran-160 kuḻi

The total extent of these lands was 3050 kuḻi and this grant was made for
providing offering and worship like a6vibali and archaṉābhōgam.

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(A.R. No. 165 of 1915.)
Kīḻputtūr, Wandiwash Taluk, North Arcot District
Svayambhunāthasvami temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḻa II: year 7: 1140 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the sale of a land as dēvādāna for providing sacred
cloths (tiruppaḍimāṟṟu) to the temple of Tirukkumarīsvaramuḍaiya Mahādēva at
Irukkalāmputtūr in Poṉnūr-nāḍu, a subdivision of Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍa
sōḻa-maṇḍalam by certain individuals of Irukkalāmputtūr village by name Ilāḍai Kumaraṉ
Murugaṉ alias Muruga Vēlāṉ, Pūttuṟai Sāttaṉ Sūriyaṉ, and Paiyabhūthi Kāmaṉ Sēri.

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(A.R. No. 166 of 1915.)
Kīḻputtūr, Wandiwash Taluk, North Arcot District
Svayambhunāthaswāmi temple-inside the same maṇḍapa
Achutadēva-Māhārāyar: Vikāri, Tai, Friday: 15th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the installation of the image of Dakshiṇāmūrti and the gift of a
tank named Guṇasīli for his worship by Ellappar, the personal servant (aḍappam) of
Achchyutadēva Mahārāya.

ccutayateva mahārāyar aṭappa[m*] mallappa nā[ya*]kkar taṉmamāka
tekṣiṇāmuṟtti nayiṉārai

viṭṭār yitukku akutam paṇṇi[ṉa*]varkaḷ keṅkaik karaiyil
kārām pacuvai taṉ kaiyāle koṉṟa pāvattile

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(A.R. No. 167 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District.
Veṅkaṭēśa Perumāḷ temple-on the west wall of the central shrine
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 11: 1023 A.D.
inscription.p:
Begins with the praśasti of the ruler tirumaṉṉi vaḷara etc., This inscription
registers a gift of 113 sheep for providing milk offerings to the deity Veṇṇaikkūttar at
Tirumukkūḍal by Nāgaṉ Sūriyaṉ, a native of Akkāramaṅgalam. The ūrār of Tirumukkūḍal
and Kūḍalūr received the sheep and took the responsibility of supplying milk and
butter to the deity of the temple. Tirumukkūḍal and Kūḍalūr mentioned as northern
hamlets of Madurāntakach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam. The latter was an independent village
(taṇiyūr) of Kaḷattūr kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍa-sōḻamaṇḍalam. A liquid measure namely
madhurāntaka-nāḻi is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(A.R. No. 168 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūdal, Madurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District.
Veṅkaṭēśa Perumāḷ temple—on the same wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 11: 1023 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription begins with the praśasti of Parakēsarivarman Rājēndrachōḻadēva
I. It records the gift of 90 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp to the temple of
Mahāvishṇu at Tirumukkūḍal, a part of Śri Madurantakach-chaturvedimaṅgalam, which
was an autonomous village (taṉiyūr) of Kaḷattūrk-kōṭṭam, a sub-division in Jayaṅgoṇḍasoḻa-
maṇḍalam, by certain Veṇkāḍaṉ Umaiyāṉ Kuḻali, a resident of Dāmōdarachēri in
Vembaṟṟūr alias Amaṉinārāyaṇach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya of Maṇṇi-nāḍu,
a sub-division of Rājēndrasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu situated on the northern bank of Chōḻa-nāḍu.

l.ta:
kkarakoṭṭamu mutirpaṭavallai maturamaṇṭalamuṅ kāmiṭai vaḷanāmaṇaik
koṇaiyum veñcilaivi-

rar pañcappaḷḷiyum pā(y)caṭaippaḻana mācuṇitecamum ayarvil vaṇ
kirtti ātinakaravaiyiṟ ca

tuk kiḷaiyoṭum piṭittup palacanattoṭu niṟaik kulatanakkuvaiyuñ
ciṭṭaruñcerimiḷai yoṭṭaviṣeyamu

ṟaicolait taṇṭapputtiyu miraṇacūraṉai muraṇukat tākkit tikkaṇai
kīrttit takkaṇalāṭamu

[nicatam ney uḻakkiṉukku vaitta cāvāmuvāp perāṭu toṇṇūṟu] ||-

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(A.R. No. 169 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District.
Veṅkaṭēśa Perumāḷ temple-on the same wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 5: 1017 A.D.
inscription.p:
This record begins with the praśasti of Parakēsarivarmaṉ Rājēndra chōḻa I. It
records the gift of 90 sheep for a lamp by certain Veḷḷāḷa Naḍaiyāḍi alias Maṇināgaṉ,
a resident of Kūḍalūr to the temple of Tirumukkūḍal-āḻvār in Madurāntakach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, which was an autonomous village (taṉiyūr) in Jayaṅgoṇḍa-sōḻa-
maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
tiruma[ṉ*]ṉi vaḷara irunilamaṭantaiyum poṟ(ṟa)ceyap
pāvaiyum cirttaṉi celviyum taṉperu

ti[yu*]ḷ ūḻiyuḷ iṭaituṟai nāṭum tuṭarvaṉavelipaṭar vaṉavāci
cuḷḷiccūḷ matiḷ koḷḷippākkaiyum naṇṇaṟkarumuraṇ maṇṇaik
kaṭa-
m veḷḷāḷa naṭaiyāṭi maṇinākaṉ vai[t*]ta tiruna

viḷakkukkum
cāvāmuvāp perāṭāka vaitta āṭu toṇṇū-

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(A.R. No. 170 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District.
Veṅkaṭēśa-Pērumāḷ temple-on the same wall
Rājēndra-Chōḷa I: year 9: 1021 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers a gift of 90 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp to the
god of Tirumukkūḍal of Śri Madhurāntakach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam by the head man
named Tikaiyaṭakki Kaṇḍaṉ of Kāmavūrkkiḷiñalūr, a northern hamlet of Śri Vāṉavaṉ
mahādēvich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, an independent village (taṉiyūr) in Āmūrk-kōṭṭam.

l.ta:
tirumaṉṉivaḷara irunila maṭantaiyum porccayap pāvaiyuñ
cirttaṉic celviyun taṉ perunteviyarāki iṉpuṟa neṭitiyalūḻiyuḷ
iṭaituṟai nāṭun tuṭarvaṉa velippaṭar vaṉavāsiyuñ cuḷḷiccūḻ
matiṭkoḷḷi-

ppākkaiyum naṇṇaṟkarumuraṇ maṇṇaikkaṭakkamum poru kaṭaliḻattaraicar
tamuṭiyum āṅkavar teviyaroṅkeḻil muṭiyum muṉṉavar pakkal
teṉṉavar vaitta cuntara muṭiyum intiraṉāṟamun teṇṭirai

īḻamaṇṭa[la*]muḻuvatu meṟipaṭaikkeraḷan muṟaimaiyiṟ cūṭuṅ
kulataṉamākiya palarpukaḻ muṭiyuñ ceṅkatirmālaiyuñ caṅkatirvelait
tolperuṅ kāvaṟ palpaḻan tivuñ ceruvic ciṉavi irupattorukā
laraicu-

kaḷai kaṭṭaparacurāmaṉ mevaruñ cāntimat tivaraṇ karuti iruttiya cempoṟ
ṟiruttaku muṭiyu[m*] payaṅkoṭu paḻimika muyaṅkiyil mutukiṭṭoḷitta
cayaciṅkaṉ aḷapperum pukaḻoṭu piṭiya[l] iraṭṭapāṭi eḻarai ila-

kkamum navanetikkulap perumalaikaḷu māpporu taṇṭāṟ koṇṭa
kopparakesarivaṉmarāṉa śrīrāje
kalattu tirumukkūṭalāḻvārkku āmūrk koṭṭattut taṉiyūr śrī vāṉavaṉ

timaṅkalattu vaṭapiṭākaik kāma[vū*]rk kiḷiñalūr
kiḻavaṉ tikaiyaṭakki kaṇṭaṉ tiruna[n*]tā viḷakkoṉṟiṉukku nicatam
ney uḻak-

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(A.R. No. 171 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District.
Veṅkaṭēśa Pērumāḷ temple-on the west and south walls of the same shrine
Rājarāja-Chōḻa I: year 28, day 141: 1013 A.D.
inscription.p:
Records that the sabhā of Madhurāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam purchased a plot
of land in the name of the deity of the temple Sri Mukkūḍalāḷvār. This sabhā also
raised a loan of thirty kaḻañju of gold from the temple treasury. It is stipulated that
the yield from the land as well as the interest accrued from the gold were to be utilized
for providing food offerings to the above deity. The mendicants (upāsakās) of the
temple undertook the responsibility of carrying out the charity. Rāman Tiruvirāmēśvaran
alias Alaṅkārādittaṉ, the madhyasta who wrote the accounts of samvatsara-vāriyam
figures as singnatory.

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 95.
(A.R. No. 172 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District.
Veṅkaṭēśa Perumāḷ temple-on the same walls
Rājēndra-Chōḷa I: Year 7, day 229: 1019 A.D.
inscription.p:
It records the detailed arrangements made by the sabhā of Madhurāntakach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam for rearing a flower garden in the name of Rajēndrasōḻaṉ, situated
to the south of the temple of Mahāvishṇu out of the taxes collected from Kūḍalūr.
The apportionment of allotted paddy is stated. Subrahmaṇya brahmapriyaṉ figures as
signatory.

inscription.head:
No. 96.
(A.R. No. 173 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District
Veṅkaṭēśa Pērumāḷ temple-on the south wall of the same shrine
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa I: Year 5: 1075 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the exemption of taxes for the lands at Kūḍalūr, near
Tirumukkūḍal by the sabhā of Śri Madhurāntakach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a taṉiyūr in
Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam, a sub-division of Jayaṅkoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam, except those already allotted
as maḍappuṟam, flower garden (nandavānappuṟam) and other lands. The said land was
gifted by the general Dharmiyaṉ Vaḍuganāthaṉ alias Vīrarājēndra Dharmapālar after
purchase. The sabhā received 500 kāśu from the donor as deposit towards taxes of
that land. At the bidding of the twelve brāhmaṇas who represented their wards, the
transaction was ordered to be engraved on stone and copperplate.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka cayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ malara malarmakaḷ
puṇarntu urimaiyil ciṟa

nattu puvana

rāna

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(A.R. No. 174 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District
Veṅkaṭēsa Perumāḷ temple-on the east wall of the same shrine
Rajēndra-Chōḻa I: year 9, day 38: 1021 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of 90 sheep by Maṇḍai Naṅgaiyār, the senior
queen (mūttaraisiyār) of Araiyaṉ Rājarājaṉ alias Vāṉavaṉ Brahmādhirājar for a perpetual
lamp. Kaḻaṉiyār [land owners] and ūrār of Nilamaikūḍalūr of Nīrkuṇṟam, a northern
hamlet of Sri Madhurāntakach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, agreed to supply uḻakku ghee
daily. Tiruvikrama Vaikundaṉ, an accountant of the temple figures as signatory.

l.ta:
tiruma[n*]nivaḷara irunila maṭantaiyum porccayap pāvaiyuñ
cirttaniccelviyu

ṉperunteviyarāki yiṉpuṟa neṭutiyalūḻi

kkaiyu naṇṇaṟ karumuruṇ maṇṇaikkaṭakkamum porukaṭaliḻat taraiyar
tamuṭiyum āṅkavar teviyaroṅkeḻil muṭiyu muṉṉavar pakkal
teṉṉavar vaitta cu

ra muṭiyu mintiranāra(va)mum teṇṭirai

koṇṭa kopparake

rājar
muttaraiciyār maṇṭai naṅkaiyār ivvāḻvārkku vaitta tiruṉa

tirumuṟṟattu nicatam uḻakku neyyaṭṭuvomāṉom ivvūr
vaṭapiṭākai nirkkuṉṟattu nilaimaikūṭalūrk kaḻaṉiyomum
ūromum ivai tevar karaṇattāṉ tiruvikkira-

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(A.R. No. 175 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District.
Veṅkaṭēśa Perumāḷ temple-on the east wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 4, day 352: 1016 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the rearrangement of the expenses for the celebrations
of new moon days (amavāsya) to the deities of Tirumukkūḍal by the assembly of
Madhurāntakach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, an independent village in Jayaṅkoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam.
It is stated that originally the provision was made for the supply of 120 kāḍi of paddy
by the ūrār for Puraṭṭāsi festival, 150 kāḍi of paddy by Rājārāja Vādyamārāyaṉ for
Aipasi festival and 120 kāḍi by the ūrār of Vaippūr out of the yield from the lands
situated in Piṇāyūr near Arumpuliyūr which was called “Tiruviḻā chatiram”. It is
further stated that the amavāsya festival includes procession, sacred bath in the river
(tīrtāmāḍal) and sacred bath in the temple (tirumañjanam). The provisions for the
offerings to Tirumukkūḍal Āḻvār, Garuḍadēvar, Sēnāpathi by Sēnāpati and Chakkarattāḻvār
were specified such as oil for lamp, various aromatic items for sacred bath and also
food offerings for four persons who were the palanquin bearers.

l.ta:
nāḷ muṉṉūṟṟaympattiraṇṭu cayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu taniyūr
śrī maturā

yom nam tirumukkūṭal āḻvār tiruviḻāc

inscription.head:
No. 99.
(A.R. No. 176 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District
Veṅkaṭēśa Perumāḷ temple-on the east wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 3: 1015 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of 3 kaḻañju of gold for the expenses of the
festival of Māsimakam in the temple of Tirumukkūḍal-āḷvar of Śri Madhurāntakach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, an independent village in Jayaṅkoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam by
Nārāyaṇakramavittaṉ, a resident of Śri Vishṇuchēri Piṇukkipuṟam and also a member
of village assembly (āluṅgaṇam). The priests of the temple agreed to provide for the
special food offerings and sacred bath on the festival day by the interest of the gold
endowed.

l.ta:
ṭalāḷvārupāsakaroṅ kaittiṭṭu ivvūr yāḷuṅkaṇattāruḷ
śrīviṣṇuccerip piṇuk-

kaiyāl yāṅkaḷ koṇṭu kaṭava poṉ
kācu niṟaikka-

llālt tuḷainiṟai mukkaḻañcu ippoṉ koṇṭa paricāvatu ivvāṭṭai
mācit tiṅka-

ḷ mutalk kaḻañciṉvāyt tiṅkaḷiraṇṭu māppoṉ policaiyāka
etirāṭṭai māciya-

poṉ oṉpatu mañcāṭikkum ēṟiluñ curuṅkilum poṉ
kaḻañciṉukku

t tūṇiyum porikkaṟiyamutu nāliṉukku ney
uḻakku nāliṉukku ney nāliṉukku ney nāliṉukku ney nāliṉukku
ney

amutu ceyya ney irunāḻi muḻākkum āka ne[y*]mutu muṉṉāḻikkum
ēṟiluñ curuṅki

k kuṟuṇiyum āka ipparicu āṇṭuvarai
mācimaka-

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. 177 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District.
Veṅkaṭēsa Perumāḷ temple-on the east wall
Būpaṇa-Uḍaiyār: Vikriti; Āḍi: 15th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. It records an order of Srimathu Maṅgappa Uḍaiyār to Pemmarasar
Attāṉai Ōbāḷu Nathar, a vassal of Būpaṇa Uḍaiyār, son of Viruppaṇa Uḍaiyār.

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. 178 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District.
Veṅkaṭēśa Perumāḷ temple-on the west, south and east walls of the same shrine
Rājarāja-Chōḻa I: year 28, day 141: 1040 A.D.
inscription.p:
This record begins with the praśasti of Rājarāja I. It records the decision taken
by the assembly of Madhurāntakach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a village in Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-
maṇḍalam which met in the hall called Sembiyaṉmahādēvi Perumaṇḍapam which was
built in the brahmasthāna of the village by the king. The sabhā agreed to remit the
taxes on certain temple lands from the interest on the gold which they received from
the temple treasury. The details of the temple lands with boundaries are specified.
Rāman Tiruvirāmīśvaraṉ alias Alaṅgārādittaṉ, the madhyasta of the Mādhavach-chēri,
who was also an accountant of the samvatsara-vāriyam and others figure as signatories.

l.ta:
tirumakaḷ polap perunilac celviyun tanakke urimai
pūṇṭamai manakkoḷak kāntaḷurccālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭuṅ
kaṅkapāṭiyu nuḷampapāṭiyun taṭikaipāṭiyuṅ kuṭamalaināṭuṅ kollamuṅ
kaliṅkamu meṇṭicai pukaḻtara vīḻamaṇṭalamu iraṭṭapāṭi Eḻarai
yilakkamu

ṇṭiṟal veṉṟit taṇṭāṟ koṇṭu taṉṉeḻil
vaḷarūḻiyu ḷellā yāṇṭun toḻutakai viḷaṅkum yāṇṭe
ceḻiyarait tecukoḷ śrīkovirājarāja rājakesa

r nirampa peruṅkuṟi kūṭiyiruntu na[m*]muṭaiya
śrīmukkūṭalā-

ṇṭārattu palicaikkuk koṇṭa poṉ mutal nūṟṟuk
kaḻañcu ippoṉ nūṟṟuk kaḻañciṟ palicai koṇṭu iṟai
iṟuppatāṉa nilam ivvāḻvār tirunāmattāl vilaikoṇṭuṭaiya
tūṇinilam ivvūr vaṭapiṭākaik kūṭalūrk kaḻaniyil

lil
meṟkaṭaiya śrīnārāyaṇaccerik kuñcappavvilk kaṇṇāṟṟut
tiruvaraikuṉṟu vatikku meṟku mutaṟcatirattu
vil kiḻakkaṭaiya ivaniṭaiye vilai koṇṭa pāṭakam

paṭṭa iṟaiyu mippoṉṉiṟ palicaiye koṇṭu inilattukku
iṟaiyiṟuttuk kuṭuppomāka

nāna alaṅkārātittaneṉ ivai e[ṉ*]ṉeḻuttu
paṇiyāl śrīkesavacceri

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. 179 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District.
Veṅkaṭēsa Perumāḷ temple-on a slab supporting a beam set up in the
inner enclosure of the temple
Nripatuṅgavarman: year 24: 893 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is incomplete. It records a gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold for a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Vishṇubhaṭārar at Tirumukkūḍal by Arikkaṇḍa-
perumānār, the son of Kāḍupaṭṭi-muttaraiyar. The assembly of Sīyapuram in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭuk
-kōṭṭam was entrusted with the gift, and they agreed to supply oil for the lamp out of
interest. As the interest being fixed by three mañjāḍi per kaḻañju, the annual interest
being calculated as four and a half kaḻañju of gold. It was also agreed that 180 nāḷi of
oil should be supplied for the interest at the rate of 40 nāḻi per kaḻañju. Sīyapuram
might be the ancient name of present Paḻaiyasīvaram near Tirumukkūḍal.
Published in S I I Vol XII No: 75.

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. No. 180 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District
Veṅkaṭēsa Perumāḷ temple-on the south wall of the first prakāra
Vikrama-Chōḻa: year 3: 1121 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is incomplete. It begins with the praśasti of Vikramachōḻa
‘pūmādu’ etc. It records the sale of land by the ūrār of Pālaiyūr alias Rājēndrasōḻanallūr
in Pālaiyūr-nāḍu, in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭu-kōṭṭam a sub division of Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam
after receiving money from the treasury of the temple. It is also stated that the land
was declared as iṟaiyili dēvadāna. The land was sold to the temple of Tiruviṇṇagara-
Āḻvār at Tiruviṇṇagar in Aṟṟūr-nāḍu for conducting various rituals and services at the
above temple.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇarap puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu
virumpa tanniru patama[lar ma]nnavar cūṭa manniya vurimaiyāl maṇimuṭi
cūṭicce-

i[nnam k]āñcippaḷḷa-

ṅkaḷin varampe piṭittu kiḻakku pona peruvaḻiyile uṟa itukkut

ti vaṭakku nokkip periyeriyuḷḷe veppañ ceriye[y]p pona
vaḻikku uṟa itukkukkiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai eriyuḷḷe vempac
ceri yeṟippona

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District
Veṅkaṭēsa Perumāḷ temple-on the east wall of the same prakāra
Vira Rājēndra-Chōla: •••: 11th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription contains only the praśasti, ‘tiruvaḷar tiṟaḷpuyattu’ etc. and stops
with the name of the king.

l.ta:
n maṇippūṇeṉat
tāṅkiya maṉmaṇikkoṟṟa veṇkuṭainiḻaṟ kuvalaiyattuyirkaḷai peṟṟa

uṟaipilattuṭai kaliyotuṅka arumpeṟaṟ ṟamaiyaṉāḷava

taṉṟiru maittuṉaṉṉākiya kaṅkaikoṇṭa co-

ḻaṉai eḻuyar yāṉaic cerapāṇṭiyaṉeṉṟiṇṭiyal maṇimuṭi yicai
peṟaccūṭṭi pāṇṭimaṇṭalaṅ kuṭuttu āṇṭakai vaṭikoṇṭa
katirvel muṭikoṇṭa coḻaṉaic cu

rac[o*]ḻaṉeṉṟeḻil
muṭicūṭṭit taṉ kiḷaiyavaravaṟkāy pukaliṭam veṟu veṟaruḷi malaikkum
vikkalaṉṟaṉṉeṭu varicilait taṭakkai mācāma

rai pukatturatti āṅkavar veṅkai naṉṉāṭṭil
miṭṭumavar viṭṭa tāṅ-

ceṟṟavaṉ cirattiṉaiyaṟuttu maṟṟavaṉṉoru makaḷākiya viru

kaiyañcāyalai mukattoṭu mukku veṟākki
mikaikatir muṉṟām vicaiyiṉum paripavan tirvaṉeṉak karutip poruvāṉ

vaṭakaṭaleṉa vakutta tāṉaiyait
taṭakkaḷi ṟoṉṟāl kalakki aṭalpuri kocalar ciṅkaṉaik koṭi(ppa)p

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(A.R. No. 182 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District.
Veṅkaṭēsa Perumāḷ temple-on the east wall of the same prakara
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 16: 1028 A.D.
inscription.p: Published in Epigraphia Indica Vol. XXI, No. 38

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(A.R. No. 183 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District
Veṅkaṭēsa Perumāḷ temple-on the east wall of the same prakara
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 16: 1028 A.D.
inscription.p:
It registers an agreement made between the officials and the priests (Vaikhānasa)
of the temple of Tirumukkūḍalāḻvār of Uttamasōḻa nilamai, a northern hamlet of Śri
Madhurāntakach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, an autonomous village (taniyūr) in Kaḷattūrk-
koṭṭam, a sub-division of Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam about the use of surplus paddy of
120 kalam from their land which was allotted to them. Arrangements were made to
appoint two persons to recite the sacred songs and to pay 120 kalam of paddy as
remuneration and four kaḻañju of gold for cloths. It is stated that this arrangement
was made of the order of Uvarkuḍi kiḻār, an official.

l.ta:
tirumaṉṉi vaḷara irunilamaṭantaiyum poṟceyaṟpāvaiyum
cirttaṉic celviyum taṉ perunteviyarāki iṉpuṟa neṭutuyaruḻiyu
ḷiṭai tuṟaināṭun tuṭarvaṉavelip paṭarvana vāciyuñ cuḷḷiccūḻ
matil koḷḷip pākkaiyum naṇṇaṟkarumuraṇ maṇṇaikkaṭa[k*]kamum
porukaṭalīḻattaraicartamuṭiyum āṅkarteviyar Oṅkeḻil muṭiyum
muṉṉavar pakkal teṉṉavaṉ vaiytta cuntaramuṭiyum intira nāramun
teṇṭirai īḻamaṇṭala muḻutum eṟipaṭaikkeraḷaṉ muṟaimaiyiṟ
cūṭuṅ kulatanamākiya palarpukaḻ muṭiyum ceṅkatir mālaiyum caṅkatir
velait tolperuṅkāvaṟ palapaḻantīvum ceruviṟ ciṉaviṟ
irupattoru kālaraicu kaḷaikaṭṭa paracurāmaṉ ••• •••• poṟ
ṟiruttuku

muṭiyum payaṅkoṭu paḻimika muyaṅkiyil mutukiṭṭoḷitta
ceya-

ciṅkaṉaḷapperum pukaḻoṭum piṭi[yal*] iraṭṭapāṭi eḻaraiyilakkamum
navanetik kulapperumalaikaḷum vikkiramavirac cakkarakoṭṭamu mutirpaṭa
vallai maturai maṇṭalamuṅ kāmiṭaivaḷaiya nāma(ṇ)ṇaik koṇaiyum

inscription.head:
No. 107.
(A.R. No. 184 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District
Veṅkaṭēsa Perumāḷ temple-on the east wall of the same prakāra
Vīra Rājēndra-Chōḻa: year 2: 1065 A.D.
inscription.p:
Begins with the praśasti of Vīra Rājēndrachōḻadēva. It records the gift of two
twilight lamps to the temple of Tirumukkūḍal-Āḻvār by a brāhmaṇa lady named
Tāntōnṟi, wife of Vārkkūra sōlai Bhuvasvasi of Marudūr in Tiruvindaḷūr-nāḍu. The
charity was placed under the supervision of Kantāḍai Sri Vāsudēvakkramavittan of
Parakēsarichēri.

l.ta:
tiruvaḷartikaḻ puyattiruni[la*]valai[ya*]m tanamaṇi pūṇena
tāṅkiya manmaṇik koṟṟaveṇkuṭainiḻaṟ kuvalaiyattuyirkaḷai
peṟṟatāyinu [peṇa*] maṟṟuḷḷaṟaikaḻalaraiaṭi niḻalotuṅka
uṟaipilattuṭai kaliyo

tuṅka muraimayi larupalat tamaiya ṉāḷavantāṉai nirumpuvi nikaḻ
rāj

rā[ja*]neṉ ṟoḷimaṇimuṭi taṉṟuṇait tampiyai taniveṟcaṉ
vallapaṉaip pillaṅkalai maturāntakaṉai coḷa

śrī vira rājēntradēvaṟku yāṇṭu 2- ṇṭāvatu coḻamaṇṭalat

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(A.R. No. 185 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District
Veṅkaṭēsa Perumāḷ temple-on the east wall of the same prakāra
Rājēndra-Chōla I: 11th c. A.D.
inscription.p: Built in. Contains only a portion of the praśasti of the king.

l.ta: teviyaroṅkeḻil muṭiyum muṉnavar pakkal teṉṉavar vaitta cu

mattivaraṇ karutiy iruttiya
cempoṉ tiruttaku muṭiyum payaṅkoṭu paḻimika •••••

ṭaivaḷai nāmaṇaikkoṇaiyum veñcilai virar pañcappaḷḷiyum
pācuṭaippaḻaṉa mācuṇitecamum ayarvi ••••

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. 187 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District
Veṅkaṭēsa Perumāḷ temple-on the dhvajastambha
Bhava, Āḍi 20: 16-17th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
It records the gift of the village Tirumukkūḍal, as sarvamāṉya and also the
assignment of taxes such as iḍaiturai, āḷamañji etc., to the temple of Śrī Veṅkaṭēsvara-
svāmi by Veṅkaṭappa-Nāyaka, according to an order issued by Rāmapaiyar at the
instance of Doḍḍayarasar.

l.ta:
śrī veṅkaṭecuvara cuvāmikku tirumu[k*]kūṭal
kirāmamum yiṭai tuṟaiyum āḷamañciyum cakalamum saṟuvamānyamāka

yinta taṉma kāriyattukku ārākilum akitam paṇṇiṉāl keṅkai
karaiyile kārāmpacuvai koṉṟa toṣattile pokakkaṭavarākavum

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. 188 of 1915.)
Tirumukkūḍal, Madhurantakam Taluk, Chingleput District
On a stone (tiruvāḻikkal) set up in a street of the same village
Vira Bukkaṇa-Uḍaiyār: 14th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Records the grant of taxes such as jōḍi, sūlavari amounting to 300 paṇam collected from the
lands (tiruviḍāiyāṭṭam) owned by the temple of Tirūvēṅkaḍamuḍaiyāṉ, to the temple
itself, for the welfare of Bukkaṇa uḍaiyār and his family.

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. 189 of 1915.)
Brahmadēśam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara Temple-on the north wall of the central shrine
Pārtivēndiravarman: year 4: 963 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of 12(1/4) kaḻañju of gold for providing food offerings and for
burning a perpetual lamp to the image of Riṣabhavāhaṉa in the temple of Śrī Pōndaip-perumāṉaḍigaḷ
of Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam at Tiruvēgambapuṟam in Dāmark-kōṭṭam, by a person
called Siṅgaperumāḷ. The mahāsabhā received the gold and sold three hundred kuḻi of
land free from taxes for this purpose and agreed to carry out the endowment out of
the yield of the land.

l.ta: perumā(ṉa)naṭikaḷukku ciṅkaperumāḷ ma ••• tiru ••• ṉ tirumeṉi iṣa

haṉa[r*]kku oru tiruvamirtu[k*]kum oru nantāviḷa[kku]kku[m iṭuvitta]
poṉ paṉṉiru kaḻañcey kāl

ippoṉ koṇṭu para[vi]pulattu merkkaṭaiya muṉṉūṟu ••• kuḻi
viṟṟu kuṭuttom

perumakka(ḷ)[ḷom] viṟṟu iṟai iḻicci kuṭu[ttom] innilam
koṇṭu orupoḻtu[t]-

ḷakkum iṟakkiṉār pāvattiṟ paṭuvār [ iddharmmam rakṣi] ppār [śrī]pādam
eṉṟalai melaṉa [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. 190 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara Temple-on the north wall
Rājarāja-Chōḻa I: year 11: 996 A.D.
inscription.p: This inscription is fragmentary. It stops with the village name.

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. 191 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramouḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Rajēndra-Chōḻa I: year 3, day 350,: 1015 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of five kaḻañju of gold for the food offerings and to provide in the mid-day
service to the temple of Śri Pōndai-mahādēva at Parākarma sōḻach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a
brahmadēya village of Karaik-kōṭṭam in Dāmark-kōṭṭam, a sub-division of Jayaṅkoṇḍasōḻa-
maṇḍalam by Indaḷadēvi, queen of Vallavaraiyar Vandyadēvar.

peruṅkuṟi perumakkaḷom eḻuttu emmur
śrī po-

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. 192 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Rājarāja-Chōḻa I: year 18: 1003 A.D.
inscription.p:
It records a gift of twelve kaḻañju of gold for a perpetual lamp to the temple
of Śrī Pōndai-Paramēśvarar at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam of Tiruvēgambapuram
in Dāmark-kōṭṭam by Kampaṉ Malaiyaṉ of Tumbūr and Nakkaṉ Pavitram alias
Rājarāja Talaikkōli. The members of the samvatsara-vāriya who looked after the village
affairs (grāmakārya) were entrusted with the maintenance of the lamp, out of the
amount of interest. Kēsava Bhaṭṭaṉ figures as a writter of this resolution.

l.ta:
ḷur cālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkai
nāṭuṅ kaṅkapāṭiyu nuḷampapāṭiyu

muṅ kaliṅkamum eṇṭicai pukaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamun tiṇṭiṟal veṉṟit
taṇṭāṟ koṇṭu taṉṉeḻil vaḷar ūḻiyuḷel-

ḻuteḻa viḷaṅkum yāṇṭe ceḻiñarait tecukoḷ
ko

m ippoṉ paṉṉiru kaḻañcukkum policaikkum eṅkaḷūr
śrīpo

vaṟ nicatam uḻakkeṇṇaiyā
lerippatākavum ivviḷakku oṉṟu

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. 193 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Pārthivēndravarmaṉ: year 13: 972 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is incomplete and damaged. It seems to register a gift for
special food offerings (peruntiruvamudhu) to Śrī Pōndaip-perumānaḍigaḷ during a particular
festival day (peruviṟṟaināḷ) by an individual called Jāthavēda-kramavittaṉ of Paṟiyalūr.

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. 194 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyār Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Pārthivēndravarmaṉ: year 13, day 120: 972 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of thirty kaḻañju of gold [tuḷai-niṟai-pōn] for two
perpetual lamps to the temple of Śri Pōndaip-perumānaḍigaḷ by Chaturan Muvāyiravaṉ,
as Madhyasthan [Trairājyaghaṭikā Madhyasthan] and also an officer who belongs to
the Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam of Tiruvēgampapuram in Dāmar-kōṭṭam.

This money has been utilized (as deposit) for making the temple land as tax-free
and from the yield of that land, the mahāsabhā agreed to maintain the perpetual lamps.
The Gaṇavāriyapperumakkaḷ undertook the responsibility of burning the two lamps.

l.ta: tuḷai niṟai muppatinkaḻañcu ip[po]ṉ muppatin kaḻañcum i
kkāṟp poṉ aṉṟāḻkovukku maṉṟap peṟuvārākavum i-

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. 195 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Pārthivēndravarmaṉ: year 2: 961 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records that all the lands belonging to the temple were made
tax-free by the village assembly añjaṣhṭasatam of Rājamalla-chaturvēdimaṅagalam in
Tiruvēgambapuram, a village in Dāmar-kōṭṭam, in lieu of the amount of gold 86(3/4)
kaḷañju which they owed to the temple. It also states that all the lands would also
be exempted from taxes. [Trairājya-ghaṭikā-madhyasthan] Mūvāyiravaṉ, figures as a
writter of this resolution.

l.ta: ṭava eṇ(m)pattaṟu kaḻañce mukkāl poṉṉum ivaruṭaiya
re irupattu aiṅkaḻañcu poṉ taṇṭamiṭuvārākavum i

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. 196 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
10-11 c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Records the Vana-nāyaka (the lord of the jungle) who is known as Pōndai-nātha
who is Aja (who is not born), who was worshipped by Agṉi at first, who was
worshipped by the dēvās in Trēta-yuga and by saints like Rēvasa in Dvāpāra-yuga, has
been installed here in Kaliyuga. The deity is smeared with sandal paste.

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. 197 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Pārthivēndravarmaṉ: year 13: 972 A.D.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of 30 kaḻañju of gold as tested and weighed by dhanmakkaṭṭaḷai
by Poṟkōyil Mūvāyiravan (alias ?) Chaturaṉ, the Trai-rājya-ghaṭika madhyastha of
Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam of Tiruvēkambapuram in Dāmaṟk-Kōṭṭam for maintaining
a brāhmaṉa, who was to workship the deity during the three sandhis and to perform
mid-night service.

l.gr:
rūyaśāḥ pūjitum brā[hma]ṇāya
triśāniṣkā suvarnnam gaṇaga[ṇa] nilayōdānnrapa syaikamanti •••

••• k koyil muvāyiravaṉ ivvū[r] śrī pon[t]ai perumāṉa[ṭi]ka[ḷai*]
tri[ sandhiyu] m arddhayāmamum abhiṣēgañ cey[yu]m brāhmaṇaṉukku
nicati •••••

vvāṇṭu gaṇav[āriya]ñ cey[yu]m [ga]ṇapperuma[kka]ḷe i]nta[ brāhmaṇaṉu]

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. 198 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻa I: year lost: 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription seems to record some gift to the temple of Śri Pōndai-Āṇdār at
Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam of Tiruvēgambapuram in Dāmark-kōṭṭam. This gift
was left in charge of the gaṇapperumakkaḷ who looked after the temple administration
[śrī kāryam].

l.ta: ti maṅkalattu śrī pontai āṇṭār tiruvuṇṇāḻikai ••• ••• •••

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. 199 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvarā temple-on the north wall
Rājarāja-Chōḻa I: year 16: 1001 A.D.
inscription.p:
Badly damaged. It Begins with the introduction Tirumagaḷpōla
perunilachelviyum etc., and registers a gift of land for two persons to recite the sacred
hymns [Tiruppadiyam] in the temple of [Śrī Pōndai-perumānaḍigaḷ] at Rājamalla-
chaturvēdimaṅagalam of Tiruvāgambapuram in Dāmark-kōṭṭam.

l.ta:
••••• lap ••• ••• •••• maṉak koḷak kāntaḷurc cālai
kalamaṟuttaruḷik kaṅkapāṭiyum nuḷampapāṭiyun taṭikaipāṭiyum veṅkai
nāṭuṅ kuṭamalai nāṭuṅ kollamuṅ kali-

••• ••••• teṇṭāṟ koṇṭu [taṉṉeḻil vaḷa] ruḻiyuḷellā
yāṇṭun toḻuteḻil vaṇaṅkum yāṇṭe ceḻiñaraittecu koḷ
ko irājarāja rājakesari[va*]

ruppatiyam pāṭuvāriruvarkku
vaitta pūmiyāvatu kiraṉpāṭi kuḻi āyiramum araiy tūrampil
muṭakku vāykkāl karai kuḻi irunūṟṟuc ciṉṉamum āka
kuḻi āyirattiru •••••

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. 200 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 4: 1016 A.D.
inscription.p: Fragmentary. Mentions Rājēndra Chōḻa.

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. 201 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvarā temple-on the north wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 8: 1020 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is much damaged and incomplete. It records gift of paddy to
the temple of Śrī Pōnḍai-uḍai[yār] at Parākrama sōḻach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya
in Dāmar-nāḍu, by certain •••• Nārakkaṉ from Kēraḷānthakach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a
brahmadēya village of Veṇnāḍu of Uyyakkoṇḍār-vaḷanādu in Chōḷa-maṇdalam for
performing sacred bath (abishēka) to the deity. It also mentions that the water was to
be brought from the well called Śrīvaikuntham of this village.
