inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. 202 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvarā temple-on the north wall
Parāntaka-Chōḷa I: year 29: 936 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of gold diadem (paṭṭam) weighing five kaḷañju
of gold to the deity Śrī Pōnḍai-bhaṭṭārakar of Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam by a
certain person called Haridāsabhaṭṭaṉ, a native of Nāraṇamaṅgalam. The gaṇapperumakkaḷ
had taken charge of this gift.

l.ta:
ṉ cantirātittavaṟ mūṉṟu cantiyum
cāttuvataṟku iṭṭa paṭṭam taṉ-

ṟuḷai kuṭiñai ka[l*]lāl ayṅkaḻa[ñ*]cu itu aḻittom
vevveṟṟu vakai irupattu nālu kāṇa-

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. 203 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīsvarā temple-on the north wall
King name not mentioned: Saka 830: 908 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of 12 kaḻañju of gold for burning a perpetual
lamp to the deity Tiruppōndaip-perumāṉaḍigaḷ at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam of
Tiruvēgambapuram by certain Kāri Ilāḍatitaṉ, who belongs to Iṉṉambar-nāḍu situated
in the northern bank of Chōḷa-nāḍu. This gift was left in charge of Gaṇapperumakkaḷ
of the temple to burn this lamp with the interest of the above-mentioned gold.

l.ta: śrīcakara yāṇṭu eṇṇūṟṟu muppatu tiruvekampapuṟattu irājamalla

ttut tiruppontaip perumāṉaṭikaḷukkuc coḻanāṭṭu vaṭakarai

ceytār ceyta pāvamaṭuvār ||-

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. 204 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-On the north and west walls
Rājāraja-Chōḻa I: year 27: 1012 A.D.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of ten kaḻañju of gold for burning a perpetual lamp to the
temple of Śri Pōndaiyāḻvār at Parākramachōḻach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a brahmadēya
village in Dāmar-nāḍu of Dāmark-kōṭṭam a subdivision of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

The ten kaḻañju was utilized to purchase ninety sheep to burn the lamp, and the
shepherds of Tillaichēry agreed to supply ghee daily to the temple. This gift was left
in charge of the gaṇapperumakkaḷ.

l.ta:
tirumakaḷ polap perunilac celviyu[ntaṉak] keyurimai
pū[ṇṭa]mai ma[ṉakk]oḷa kā

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. 205 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḻīśvara temple-on the west wall of the same shrine.
Pārthivēndravarmaṉ: year 4: 963 A.D.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of land 1018 3/4 kuḻi for the food offering during the three
sandhis and to perform mid-night service and a perpetual lamp to the deity Śri
Pōndaip-perumāṉaḍigaḻ at Rājamalla-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tiruvēgambapuram, by
Kāṭṭamayyaṉ, son of Chiṉṉamayyaperkaḍai after purchasing it from Tiruvēṅgaḍamādhava-
Sarvakrutuyājiyār. The sabhā made that land as tax-free and Nāthagaṇappōsaṉ
Ēḻāyiravaṉ figured as signatory.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. 206 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷiśvara temple-on the west wall
Rājakēsarivarman: year 17: 974 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is probably an inscription of Sundara-chōḻa. It records an endowment of two plots of
tax-free land, one after purchase and the other after exchange, left incharge of the gaṇavāriya-
perumakkaḷ of the temple of Śri-Pōndaip-perumāḷ at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, by Bhāratvāji
Sadāśivan of the tiruvuṇṇāḻigai (central shrine) of Tiruvēgambam at Kachchippēḍu (Kāñchīpuram)
for the supply of sandal paste to the god.

p.i: Published in SII. Vol. XIII., No. 274

l.ta: ṭi kaḻañcukku muppatiṉ kāṭi vaṇṇam va-
nta poṉ kaḻañcarai itu tiṅkaḷo pā-

edition.l: kaṇavāriyaromai panmāh[ ēśvarare]-

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. 207 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīsvara temple-on the west wall
Rāshṭrakūṭa Kṟishṇa III: year 17: 956 A.D.
inscription.p:
It records that Marudānmaiya-Vīrasuta Bhōgavarddhana-vinagata Śri Lakshaṇa
Suta Srinarasingayyan made a gift of 21 kaḻañju of eḍarpuram gold for burning
a perpetual lamp to Śrī Pōndaip-perumāṉaḍigaḷ at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Tiruvēgambapuṟam in Dāmark-kōṭṭam.

The gaṇapperumakkaḷ of the temple received the amount and agreed to burn
the lamp out of the interest of the endowed amount.

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. 208 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīsvarā temple-On the west wall
Pārthivēndravarmaṉ: year 6: 965 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription records the gift of 440 kuḻi of land for feeding two brāhmaṇās
who were well versed in vēdas during the midday, by the assembly of añjaṣṭasam of
Tiruvēgambapuram in Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam of Dāmark-kōṭṭam. The sabhā
members took this decision at a meeting held during the day time in the big maṇḍapa
of this village and placed this charity under the members of the committee called
gaṇavāriyam. It is also stated that one among the Brahmins to be fed before the food
offering made to Paramēśvara, the deity of the temple. Āyirattirunūṟṟuva
Tikai[ttiṟa]lōttamaṉ figures as signatory.

l.ta:
rkku yāṇṭu āṟāvatu tāmarak

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. 209 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīsvarā temple-On the west wall
••• kesarivarmaṉ (Chōḻa): year 3: 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. Seems to record something done by Tiruvoṟṟiyūr Śivan, a
Śivabrāhmaṇa one among the tiruvuṇṇaḻikaiyuḍaiyār of Sri Pōṇdaiyaṇḍār.

āvatu •••• tāmark
koṭṭattu tiruvekampuṟattu irājamallac ca-

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. 210 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīsvarā temple-on the west wall.
Rājarāja-Chōla I: year 2.: 10•• A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged and incomplete. It records a gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of
gold for burning a perpetual lamp to the temple of Śrī Pōndaiyuḍaiya-mahādēva at
Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tiruvēgambapuram in Dāmark-kōṭṭam, by Indala
Dēviyār, queen of Vallavaraiyar Vanthiyadēvar. [see No 113 above]

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. 211 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara Temple-on the west wall
King name not mentioned: Śaka 830: 908 A.D.
inscription.p:
It records the exemption of tax by of añjashṭasatam the sabha of Rājamallach-
chaturvedimaṅagalam at Tiruvēgambapuram for the land called Amaraṅkuḻi which was
bought by Sivakkuṟi Kāmaḍiperumāṉ and his younger brother Perumāḷ from the sabhā
of kammāḷas and donated for providing 2 nāḻi of rice daily to Sri Pōndai-Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. 212 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīsvarā temple-on the west wall.
King name not mentioned: Saka 830: 908 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers a gift of 12(1/2) kaḷañju of gold for burning a perpetual
lamp to the temple of Tiruppōndaip-perumānaḍigaḷ at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
by Kaṇakkāyaṉ Sāttan Kaṇavati alias ••• ••• Pēraraiyaṉ, a native of Kōṭṭu ••••• in
Seṅgāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam. This gift was left in charge of the gaṇapperumakkaḷ of the temple
who agreed to burn the lamp with the interest of the above mentioned gold.

lattut tirup •••• rumānaṭikaḷukku ceṅkāṭṭuk koṭṭattuk koṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. 213 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷiśvara temple-on the west wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻa I: year: ••• : 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of twenty kaḻañju of gold by Chōḻasikhāmaṇi
Pallavaraiyaṉ of Nelvēli in Iṅga-nāḍu a subdivision of Chōla-nāḍu for burning a
perpetual lamp. The members of the tank committee (ērivāriyapperumakkaḷ) agreed to
provide oil for burning the lamp.

l.ta:
rupatiṉ kaḻañcum koṇṭu cantirātittavat oru nontā viḷakku eri ••
• tinamaiyil ērivāriyap peru-

makkaḷe niccam uḻakkeṇṇaiyāka tiṅkaḷ eḻunāṭuri e •• ṇai
ivveṇṇai muṭṭāme

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. 214 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷiśvara temple-on the west wall
King name lost: year 20: 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. It registers a gift of tax-free land (287(1/2) kuḻi) by Tiruvadi, a native
of Puduppaḷḷi for conducting four services in a day in the temple of [Śri Pōndai]
Mahādēva at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tiruvēgambapuram.

l.ta:
••• teṟkkilp pulam vaittāṉ paṭiūr [mecā] ūr cattipaṭṭaṉ
[a]

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷiśvara temple-on the west wall
Pārthivēndravarmaṉ: year lost: 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary and Damaged. It seems to register a gift of land (350 kuḻi) for
flower garden.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] kopārtthivē[ ndrādhipati] varmma[rk*]ku yāṇṭu •••

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. 216 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷiśvara temple-on the west and south walls
Rājarāja-Chōḻa I: year 29 day 10: 1014 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription begins with the introduction, ‘tirumagaḷ pōla’ etc. It records
a gift of 90 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp by Tiruppōndai of Paṟiyalūr, one of
the members of the āḷuṅgaṇam of Rajamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam. A shepherd of
this village received the gift and agreed to supply ghee daily to the temple.

kalama-

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 217 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvarā temple-on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻa I: Year 37: 944 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is incomplete. It records a gift of 14(1/2) kaḻañju of gold for
burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Śri Pōndai-perumānaḍigaḷ by certain
Mahādēva bhaṭṭaṉ, son of Maṇisivabhaṭṭa Sarvakratukkaḷ of Kūḍal, one of the members
of āḷuṅgaṇam of Rajamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tiruvēgambapuram, a village in
Dāmark-kōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 218 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻa I: Year 33: 940 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of 12 kaḻañju of gold for perpetual lamp to the
temple of Śrī Pōndaip-perumānaḍigaḷ, by Aḻagasarma Kramavittaṉ son of Maṇiśiva
Bhaṭṭa Sarvva-krathukkaḷ of Kūḍal, a member in āḷuṅgaṇam of Rājamallach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tiruvēgambapuram in Dāmar-kōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 219 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷiśvara temple-on the south wall
••• Kesarivarmaṉ (Chōḻa): year 26: 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
It is damaged and incomplete. This record registers a gift of gold for a perpetual
lamp at the temple of Tiruppōndaip-perumānaḍigaḷ by a member of the āḷuṅgaṇam of
Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

l.ta:
•• tiruppontaip perumāṉaṭikaḷukku oru na[n*]tā viḷakkerippataṟkkuk
kuṭutta poṉ •••

•••• kaḻañcinālum vanta palicai koṇṭu oru na[n*]tā viḷakku
erippatāka vaitteṉ i
ittiruviḷakkukkey vaḻiyum ivare vaitta poṉ iru kaḻañcu ||-

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall.
King name and regnal year not mentioned: 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of two kaḻañju of gold for feeding two brāhmaṇas
on new-moon days (amāvāsya) by certain Nalluḻāṉ Perumāṉ of Vaḷḷivayam. The
gaṇapperumakkal (the body) of Thiruppōndai agreed to maintain it from the interest
of the gold.

l.ta: vaitta poṉ irukaḻañcu ippoṉṉukkup palicaiāka amāvā

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 222 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻa I: Year 25: 932 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of gold for a perpetual lamp to the temple of
Śrī Pōndaip-perumānaḍigaḷ, at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tiruvēgambapuram
by certain Gaṇyaṉ Iraṇasarmaṉ, a native of Māṅgāḍu. The temple committee named
gaṇapperumakkaḷ was left in charge of burning the lamp with the interest accrued from
the donated gold.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 223 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvarā temple-on the south wall
Pārthivēndravarmaṉ: year 2: 961 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers a gift of fifteen kaḻañju of gold for a perpetual lamp
to the temple of śrī Pōndaip-perumāṉaḍigaḷ by Iṉnāṅgūr alias Alaṅkāra cheṭṭi, son of
Maṅgalapriyaṉ Kalivilaṅki, a merchant of Tiruvēgambachcheri in Rājamallach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam. The gaṇapperumakkaḷ undertook the responsibility of maintaining
the lamp with the interest.

l.ta:
yaṉ kalivilaṅki makaṉ iṉnāṅkūr
ākiya ala-

ṅkāra ceṭṭi pontaip perumāṉaṭikaḷukku oru nuntā viḷakkukku
vaitta poṉ pati-

ppār śrīpātam eṉṟalai melaṉa aṟamaṟaveṟka
aṟamallatu tu-

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 224 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara Temple-on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻa I: year 17: 924 A.D.
inscription.p:
It records a gift of 325 kuḻi of land by Aṭṭamūrti, son of Pēraiyya Nalluḻāṉ
Ūraḍi, a resident of Saṅkarappāḍi at Rajāmallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, to the deity
Dakshiṇāmūrtti for providing food offerings and worship. It is also stated that the said
land was situated in south of Paramēsvara Vāykkāl, was purchased by his father from
Naṟaiyūr Chaṇḍiśarmanār.

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 225 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara Temple-on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻa I: year 38: 945 A.D.
inscription.p:
Unfinished. It seems to record a gift by Mūtta Kuñjiramallaṉ, a resident of
Kaṟkuḍi in Maḻanāḍu on the northern bank of Kāviri, to the temple of Śrī Pōndaip-
perumānaḍigaḷ, at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam at Tiruvēgambapuram in Dāmar-
kōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 226 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara Temple-on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻa I: Year 16: 923 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the decision (vyavasthai) made by the aiñjashṭṭasata
sabhaiyār regarding the remuneration for the arbitrator (madhyasta) who was entrusted
with the work of writing the account for the tank committee (ērivāriyam). He was
to receive 4 nāḻi of paddy daily, 7 kaḻañju of gold and one pair of dress (kūṟai)
annually. On the day of submitting accounts he was expected to prove his honesty
by holding a red-hot iron rod (maḻu) in his hand. If he came out without injury,
hence also proved as pure he would be honoured with the bonus (pādasēsha). On the
other hand, if he burnt his hand he would be considered as corrupt and be fined 10
kaḻañju, but of course without any bodily punishment (sarīra-taṇḍam).

edition.l: māṉom ay[ ñcaṣṭa] śattu

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 227 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Pallava Kampavarman: year 20: 866 A.D.
inscription.p: Published in SII Vol XII, No: 107

This inscription records the gift of 11 kaḻañju of gold for supplying water to the
temple of Tiruppōndaip-perumāṉaḍigaḷ at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, by a member
of āḷuṅgaṇam of Kāvitippākkam in Paḍuvūrk-kōṭṭam.

kovicaiya kampavarmarkku yāṇṭu irupatā[vatu] paṭuvūrk
koṭṭattuk kāvati-

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 228 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
King name not mentioned: Śaka 830: 908 A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in. It records a gift of 12(1/2) kaḻañju of gold to burn a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Tiruppōndaip-perumānaḍigaḷ at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.
Gaṇapperumakkaḷ of this temple were entrusted to look after the maintenance of this
lamp with the interest drawn out of it.

l.ta:
cakarayāṇṭu eṇṇūṟṟu muppatu tiruvekampappuṟattu rājamallac

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 229 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻa I: year 1[3]: 9[20] A.D.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and incomplete. It seems to record a gift made by
a lady to the temple of Śri Pōndaip-perumānaḍigaḷ at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
in Tiruvēgambapuram.

edition.l: vatu tiruvekampappuṟattu rājamalla[ ccatu] rvēdi maṅ-

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 230 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple—on the south wall
Rājakēsarivarman: year 27: 898 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged. It seems to record a gift of 10 kaḻañju of gold for burning a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruppōndaip-perumāṉaḍigaḷ at Rājamalla-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam by Aṭṭamūtti, a resident of the village, and an endowment of land
by his wife (name lost), after purchase, for the daily feeding of a brāhmaṇa in the
temple. This inscription has been assigned to Āditya I on account of the high regnal
year of the king and the identity of the donor with the one figuring in No. 224 of 1915
of the time of Parāntaka I (M.E.R. 1916 II. 9). It is surmised that the village owed its
origin to Rājamalla an early king of the Western Gaṅga dynasty (See No. 292 above).
Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIII, No. 322

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. 231 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple—on the south wall
Parakēsarivarmaṉ: year 4: 911 A.D.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It seems to record a gift of 15 kaḻañju of gold for a perpetual
lamp, and another 75 kaḻañju for some purpose the nature of which is not known, in
the temple at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.
Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX: No. 100

l.ta:
taiñ[cu*]kaḻañ[cu*]māka ūrkkaṟcemmai toṇṇūṟṟuk kaḻañcu itaṉāl
vaṉta •••

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 232 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple—on the south wall
Parakēsarivarmaṉ: year 4: 911 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 15 kaḻañju of tested gold (ūrkaṟ-chemmai) for a twilight lamp
in the temple by Niṉṟayil Irushikēsuvaṉ of Kuṇḍiṉa-gōtra, a resident of Vāyiṉāṉallūr
in Puliyūrk-kōṭṭam.
Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX, No. 101

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 233 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻā I: year 2: 909 A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in. This inscription records a gift of land to the temple of Sri Pōndaip-
perumāṉaḍigaḷ to beat of the drum daily during the Śribali service.

edition.l:
[svastiśrī [||*] matiraikoṇ] ṭa kopparakecarivarmmaṟku yā[ṇṭu 2 āvatu]

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. 234 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple—on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻā I: year lost: 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of gold for perpetual lamp to the temple of Śrī
Pōndaip-perumāṉaḍigaḷ by Naṟṟuḻāṉ Nakkerumāṉ, native of Ilaṅkāḍu in Veṇkkuṉṟak-
kōṭṭam. The gaṇapperumakkaḷ were entrusted with the responsibility of carrying out
this charity.

l.ta:
maṅkalattu śrīpo[ntaip
perumā]ṉaṭikaḷ śrīkoyilil veṇkuṉṟakkoṭṭattilaṅkāṭṭil

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. 235 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple—on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻā I: year 25: 932 A.D.
inscription.p:
Records that Sembiyaṉ Kiḻār-nāṭṭukkōṉ a headman of Kiḷinallūr in Kiḻārk-
kūṟṟam in Sōḻa-nāḍu gifted 15 Kaḷañju of poṉ for burning a perpetual lamp with one
uḻakku oil to god Śrī Pōndai Māhadēva bhāṭṭārakar at Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
in Dāmark-kōṭṭam on the occasion of solar eclipse. The ūrār and the sabhaiyār of
Vadiyūr in Nīrvēḷūr-nāḍu in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam, who received the gift agreed to
measure out one uḻakku of oil daily to the temple.

l.ta: ṉṉāṟ poliūṭṭut tevar uḻakkiṉāl nicati uḻa-
ṇṇai muṭṭil ceṟṟa āḷukku nicati iraṇṭu co-

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. 236 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple—on the south wall
Parakēsarivarman: Year lost: 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
The beginning and end portions are lost in this inscription. It seems to record
the sale of land by Aiñjashṭasata-sabha of Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Tiruvēgambapuram to some body in Umbaḷa-nāḍu, a sub-division in Maḻa-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. 237 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Rājakēsarivarman: year 20: 891 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records an endowment of a piece of land under the tank
Tigaittiṟal-ēri by Tiruppōndai-Somāsiyar (Sōmayājiyār) son of Kumāraḍi-Vājapējiār of
Maṇarpākkam, a member of the āḷuṅgaṇam of Rājamallach-chaturvedimaṅgalam of
Tiruvēgambapuram in Dāmar-kōṭṭam, for the daily feeding of a brahman at mid-day in
the temple Srīpōndaip-perumāṉaḍigal. The gift was entrusted to the gaṇavāriya-perumakkaḷ
of the temple. This is possibly an inscription of Āditya-I.
Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIII No. 292

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. 238 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
[Parāntaka-Chōḻā I: year 5: 912 A.D.]
inscription.p:
Built in at the right side and the end portion is damaged. It records a gift of
90 sheep to burn a lamp with one uḻakku of oil daily.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 239 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻā I: year Lost: 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record some arrangement made by Añjashṭasatattu-mahasabhai
of Rājamallach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam. The remuneration was mentioned as the cloth
(kūṟai) and wage (koṟṟu) given by gaṇavāriyapperumakkaḷ. It is also recorded if there
is any default a fine of 108 kāṇam were to be remitted.

l.ta:
ppāṉ •• ṭippārkaḷ ākavum kaṇakkuc ce ••••• kku kaṇavāriyap
perumakkaḷ tanta kūṟaiyum koṟṟume koṇṭitum ••••

itaṉṟeṉṟāṉ śrī pottakappaṭiyeṭu ••• ••• •••• nūṟṟeṭṭukkāṇam
taṇṭam paṭuvārākavum ipparicu oṭṭiṉom a ••• •••

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. 240 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Parāntaka-Chōḻā I: year lost: 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. XIX No. 253

This is damaged and built in at the right end. It seems to record a gift of gold by Sōmāsi Sāṉi for
burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Śrī Pōndaip-[perumānaḍigaḷ] at Rājamallach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, besides a land gift.

l.note: Read- eṭarpuram • eḍarpuram is one type of ṅold. see inscription no. 129.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. 241 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Pārtivēndravarman: year 12, day 184: 971 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription records the gift of 525 kuḻi of tax free land by Pōttan Maḍuvuḍaiya
Veḷiyaṉ Nuḷambar Viśuddhamati Paṇḍita for the sacred bath of the God, Śrī Pōndaip-
perumāṉaḍikaḷ, three times (santis) in the day and midnight (ardhayāma) by a person
well-versed in Vēdas. The Gaṇvāriyapperumakkaḷ were entrusted with the responsibility
of executing the charity.

l.ta:
tipaṉmaṟki yāṇṭu paṉṉiraṇṭu nāḷ nūṟṟu
eṇpattunālu tāmark koṭṭattu tiruvekampapuṟattu

irājamalla catuṟvetimaṅkalattu śrīpontaiy perumāṉaṭikaḷukku

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Pārtivēndravarman: year 13: 972 A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in. It seems to record the gift of gold for burning the lamp to the
temple of Sri Pōndaip-perumā[naḍigaḷ]

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. 243 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 5, day 230: 1017 A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged at the end. Begins with the praśasti of the king, tirumaṉṉi vaḷara.
The inscription records a gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp to the temple of Sri
Pōndai Mahādēva in Parākramasōḷa-chaturvedimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Karaikkōḍu
a part of Tiruvēgambapuram of Dāmark-kōṭṭam, a sub division in Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-
maṇḍalam by Mandarakōvanār Kundādēviyār, queen of Vallavaraiyar Vandiyadēvar.

l.ta:
mum porukaṭaliḻattaracar tammuṭiyum ā[ṅka]var teviyaroṅkeḻil
mu[ṭiyu muṉṉavar pakkal] tennavar vaitta cuntara muṭiyu mintiranāramu-

teṇṭirai īḻamaṇṭala muḻuvatu māpporu taṇṭāṟ koṇṭa
kopparakecari pa

edition.l: varkku uṭaiyār vallavar[ aiyar vandya] dēvar

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. 244 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the west wall of the maṇḍapa
in front of the same shrine
Rājēndra-Chōḻa II: year 5: 1057 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the praśasti of the king. It records the sale of land
to the temple of Bhavarudra Sōmīsvaradēva on the southern bank of the river Vēhkā
[Vēgavati] by the assembly of Parākramasōḻach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Dāmar-nāḍu, a
subdivision of Dāmark-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam, for 10 kāsu from the
temple authorities. The land was also declared as taxfree. It also confers the privilege
of irrigating the land and prohibited others from drawing water. Four hundred kuḻi of
house-site was also given to the tillers for their residence.

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. 245 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall of the same maṇḍapa
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 25, day 330,: 1037 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records that the revenue official Madisūdaṉ Ādittaṉ of Siruvayalūr
deposited 7(1/2) kaḻañju and 2 mañjāḍi of gold with the authorities of the temple Śri
Pōndaiuḍaiya Mahādēva of Parākramachōḻach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Dāmar-nāḍu, a
subdivision of Dāmark-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam. The temple officials made
this gift for constructing the tirumaṇḍapa at the temple. However, the temple
authorities utilised the interest accrued from the amount, 45 kalam and one tūṇi of
paddy for conducting festivals and for various offerings and worship on every Sunday
and conducting rituals to the gods Pōndai Perumānaḍigaḷ Chandrasēkharadēvar and
Goddess. The accountant Veṅgāvaṉ Tiruvikiramaṉ alias Brahmapriyaṉ figures as signatory.

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. 246 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 30: 1042 A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription probably belongs to Rājēndra Chōḻa I. Hence it is
engraved in continuation of the above record (ARE-1915, No. 245). It seems to record
the purchase of 3000 kuḻi of land as tax-free by the Śivabrāhmaṇas from Āḷakhāṉa
kramavittan for providing food offerings to the god Sri Pōndaiyuḍaiya-mahādēva at
Parākramasōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in [Dāmark]-kōṭṭam, a subdivision of Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-
maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. 246-A of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
11th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription records the sale of land free from all taxes to the
temple of Sri Pōndaiyuḍaiya-Mahādēva by the assembly of the brahmadēya village.

l.ta:
•••• māka ikkuḻi viḷainilattukku vaṭakkuk kāṭutirutti vaicca kuḻi

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. 247 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyār Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Rājādhiraja-Chōḻa: year 33: 1051 A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in at the end. Begins with the praśasti, tiṅgaḷēr taru. It records the sale
of 3549 kuḻi of land as maḍapuṟam to the maṭha called Pavithramāṇikka maḍam
located in the premises of the Śrī Pōndaiyāṇḍār temple. The land was also freed from
other taxes and declared as iṟaiyili. They also agreed not to levy any tax from the
cultivators. The madhyastha of the village Sri Pōndai Ādichaṇḍēsvara perumāḷ figures
as signatory.

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. 248 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the south wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 3[0]: 104[2] A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in at the end. Begins with the introduction tirumaṉṉivaḷara etc. The
inscription is left incomplete after the name of the king and regnal year.

l.ta:
tirumaṉṉivaḷara irunilamaṭantaiyum porc ceyappāvaiyum
cirttaṉic celviyun taṉ perunteviyarāki iṉpuṟa neṭutuyar ūḻiyuḷ
iṭaituṟai nāṭum tuṭarvaṉavelipaṭar vaṉavāciyuñ cuḷḷiccūḻa
matiṭkoḷḷippākkai[yum naṇṇar]kkarumuraṇ maṇṇaikkaṭakkamum
porukaṭaliḻattaraiyar tamuṭiyum āṅkavar teviyaroṅkeḻil muṭiyum
•••••

ṇṭirai īḻamaṇṭala muḻuvatu[m*] eṟipaṭai keraḷan
muṟaimaiyil cūṭum kulataṉamākiya palar pukaḻ muṭiyum ceṅkatir
mālaiyum caṅkatir velait tolperuṅkāval palpaḻattivum
cerule

ciṉavillirupattorukā[llarai]cukaḷai kaṭṭa paracurāmaṉ
[melvaruñcānti]maṟṟivaraṇ ka[ruti] iruttiya cempo ••• ••••

[mu]tukiṭṭoḷitta cayaciṅkaṉ aḷapperumpukaḻoṭum piṭiyil
iraṭṭapāṭi ēḻaraippukaḻ ma[n*]navare kulapperumalaikaḷum
vikkiramavirar cakkarakoṭṭam mitur vaṭavallai maturamaṇṭalamu keṭalarum
kāmiṭai vaḷaiy nāma[ṇaik] koṇaimum veñcinavirar pañcapaḷḷiyum
pācaṭaippaḻaṉa mācuṇi tecamu[m*] ayarvil vaṇkirtti •••••

ṉai viḷaiyamarkaḷattuk kiḷaiyoṭum piṭittup palataṉattoṭuniṟai kulataṉak
kukaiyuṅ kiṭṭaruñ ceṟimiḷai oṭṭavi

yamum pūcurarcer nalkkocalai

alaikaṭal naṭuvuḷ palakalañceluttic caṅkirāma vicaiyottuṅka
paṉmaṉākiya kaṭārattaraicaṉai vākaiyam porukaṭal kumpakkariyo ••

toraṇamumo[y*]ttoḻiṟ puṉaimaṇipputavamuṅ kanamaṇikkatavamu
niṟai ••••• [tuṟai]nirp paṇṇaiyum [vaṉmalai]yil yiṟṟeṉmalaiyūrum
āḻkaṭal akaḻ cūḻmāyiruṭiṅkamu[m kala]ṅkā vanviṉai ilaṅkā

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. 249 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple: on the south wall
Rājādhirāja-Chōḻa I: year 32: 1050 A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged and Incomplete. Begins with the praśasti of the king ‘tiṅgaḷēr peṟa
vaḷar’ etc. It seems to record a meeting of the members of the sabhā at the temple
of Śri Pōndai-āḻvār and also refers to the burning of lamp before Sapthamātrukas by
Ādittakramavittaṉ, Tiruvēgambakramavittaṉ and Mādhavakramavittaṉ.

l.ta:
tiṅkaḷerpeṟa vaḷaraṅkatirk kaṭavuṭṭolkulam viḷaṅkat
to[ṉ]ṟi vaṭaticai kaṅkaiyun teṉṟicai ilaṅkaiyum kuṇaticaik
kaṭāramum kuṭaticai makotaiyun taṇṭāṟ koṇṭa tātai taṉ
veṇkuṭai niḻaṟkiḻ taṉkuṭai niḻaṟṟi ticaitoṟuñ ceṅkol
naṭāttit tikaimuṭi • •

ḷurccālai kalamaṟuppittu(kkeḻu) jayaṅkoṇṭa
coḻaneṉ matikeḻu kovirāja[kesa]ri

pperumakkaḷom emmūr tiru[ppontai āḻvār koyilile] kūṭṭak
kuṟaivaṟak kūṭiyiruntu

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. 250 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīsvara temple-on the south wall
Rājādhirāja-Chōḻa I: year 3[1]: 104[9] A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in at the left. It registers the sale of land which was freed from taxes to
the temple by the ūrār for feeding Śrī māhēsvaras, who come to worship during the
days of festivals in the temple of Sri Pōndaiuḍaiya Mahādēva of Parākramasōḻach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam of Dāmar-nāḍu in Dāmark-kōṭṭam, a sub division of Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-
maṇḍalam.

It refers to the temple of Sōḻakēraḷa-Viṇṇagar Āḻvār, while stating the boundaries
of the land.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. 251 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple: on the south wall
Rajā[dhi]rāja-Chōḻa I: year 31: 1049 A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in and damaged. It records the sale of 46 kuḻi of land for nine kaḻañju of
gold by the ūr assembly of Omānaṇḍattūr to temple of Sri Pōndaiuḍaiya Mahādēva in
order to provide food offerings to the devotees who thronged for worship to the
temple on the occasion of festival days. The land was declared as tax free.

l.ta:
kopparakecaripaṉmarāṉa uṭaiyār śrīrācā[ti*]rāca tevarkku
yāṇṭu muppattoṉṟāvatu kāliyūrk koṭṭattu sakāyirampe[ṭu]
nāṭṭu mo-

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. 252 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīsvara temple-on the south wall
Rājārāja-Chōḻa III: year 31: 1247 A.D.
inscription.p:
This record could be assigned to Rājarāja III on account of the high regnal
year. Registers a gift of golden diadem weighing 20 kaḻañju and one mañjāḍi by an
individual Aḻiyātiraṉ Viḍaiyiṉ-mēlvaruvāṉ belonging to a village in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu of
Maṇayiṟ-kōṭṭam. The gold had been equated to madirāntakaṉ māḍai and Īḻakkāsu.

l.ta:
rācarāca tevarkku yāṇṭu muppattoṉṟāvatu maṇaiyil
koṭṭattu paṭuvūr nāṭ ••• uloka •••••

aḻiyāttiṟaṉṉe[ṉ*] viṭaiyiṉ melvaruvāṉ iṭṭapaṭṭam irupatiṉ
kaḻañce mañcāṭi matirāntakaṉmāṭaiyoṭokku miḻaṉ
irupatakkiñce

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. 253 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīsvara temple-on the east wall of the same maṇḍapa
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 23: 1035 A.D.
inscription.p:
This records the sale of 1225 kuḻi of land by the mahāsabhā of Parākramasōḻa
-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Dāmar-nāḍu in Dāmark-kōṭṭam to Vāṭkai Kārikuḍi Kārikuḍaiyāṉ
of Veṟṟiyūr in Sōḻamaṇḍalam for eight kaḻañju of gold in order to provide food
offerings to the deity Subrahmaṇyadēva (who is in the west of the village). Kāmakōṭi
Bhaṭṭa of Pākkam, Chēttasanma-kramavittaṉ of Māṅgāḍu and others figure as signatories.
A post script at the end of the inscription records the Śivabrāhmanā of Subrahmaṇyadēvar
alias Sōḻakēraḷa temple of the same village received 100 kuḻi and agreed to burn a
perpetual lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. 254 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the east wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 22: 1034 A.D.
inscription.p:
Begins with the praśasti of the Chōḻa king Rājēndra I, tirumaṉṉivaḷara. It
records the gift of 12 kaḻañju of gold by Mādēvi Sāṉi, wife of Tāyapirāṉ Kramavittaṉ,
a native of Eṭṭukkūr and also a member in āḷuṅganam of Vāyalūr in Kāyirambēḍu-
nāḍu, in Kāliyūr-kōṭṭam, a subdivision of Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam for burning a
perpetual lamp.

The peruṅkuri-mahāśabhā of Parākramasōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a Karaikkōṭṭu
Brahmadēyam in Dāmar-nāḍu earmarked the plot of tax free land and agreed to
measure out thirty kalam of paddy by way of interest at the temple treasury for the
said purpose. The land was also freed from taxes.

l.ta:
tirumaṉṉi vaḷara irunila maṭaṉtaiyum poṟ
ceyappāvaiyuñ cīrttaṉic celviyum taṉ peru[n*]teviyarākiyiṉ
puṟa neṭutiyalūḻiyuḷḷiṭaituṟaināṭu[n*] tuṭar vaṉavelip paṭar vaṉa
vāciyuñ cuḷḷiccūḻ matiḷ koḷḷippākkaiyum naṇṇaṟ karumuraṇ
maṇṇaikkaṭakkamum porukaṭal īḻattaraicar tam muṭiyu māṅkavar
teviyaroṅkeḻil muṭiyum muṉṉavar pakkal teṉṉavar vaiytta
cu

raṉāramu teṇṭirai īḻamaṇṭala muḻuvatumeṟipaṭaik

ceṅkatirmālaiyum caṅkatirvelait tolperuṅkāvaṟ palpaḻa

maṟṟivaraṇ karuti iruttiya cempoṟṟiruttaku muṭiyum
payaṅkoṭu paḻimika muyaṅkiyil mutukiṭṭoḷitta cayaciṅkaṉaḷapparum
pukaḻoṭum piṭi[yil*] iraṭṭapāṭi yeḻarai yilakkamum navanetik

ātinakaravaiyil ca

rataraṉai viḷaiyamarkkaḷattuk

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. 255 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the east wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year •••: 11th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. The inscription is built in between the lines 3 & 4 and at the end. It
records the provision made for feeding 100 Śrīvaishṇava pilgrims in the hall named
Jananātha maṇḍapa who were going to the temple of Tirumala and returning after
taking sacred bath (tīrtamāḍal) on newmoon days, on the day of Tiruvōṇam festival in
the month of Puraṭṭāsi and six days festival in the month of Aipasi. It could be
inferred that this record belonged to the Vishṇu temple i.e. Sōḻakēraḷa-viṇṇagar of this
village [see no. 250] since it pertained to Śrivaishṇavas, Tiruvōṇam, Tirumala etc.

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. 256 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the east wall of the maṇḍapa
Rājēndra-Chōḻa II: year 15: 1067 A.D.
inscription.p:
A portion of the inscription is lost during the renovation of the temple. Begins
with the prasaśti, tirumagaḷ maruviya etc., of the king. Registers the sale of land to
the temple of Rudrasōlai mahādēva situated on the southern bank of Vehkā at
Parākramasōḻa-chaturvēḍimaṅgalam in Dāmar-nāḍu, a sub-division of Dāmar-Kōṭṭam by
the assembly of Veṅkuḷattūr alias Paramēsvara-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Kaḻumala-nāḍu, a
sub division of Kāliyūrk-kōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. 257 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the east wall of the maṇḍapa
Rājēndra-Chōḻa II: year 3, day 133: 1055 A.D.
inscription.p:
Beginning badly damaged. It records the decision to purchase 1126 kuḻi of land
in two pieces in the name of Ādichaṇḍēsvara taken by Mahāsabha in the meeting held
in the temple maṇḍapa for the expenses of worship (archanābhōga). Boundaries of
both lands are also mentioned. Names of channels such as Manaṟpākka-vati, Paramēsvara-
vāykkāl and Rājamallapperu-vati are mentioned.

nāḷ nūṟṟu muppattu muṉṟināl jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa maṇṭalattu •••

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. 257-A of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the same wall
Rājādhirāja-Chōḻa I: year lost: 11th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. This inscription begins with the meykīrttī Tiṅgaḷēr taru etc., It
records the sale deed of 4000 kuḻi of land executed by peruṅkuṟiperumakkaḷ of
Parākrama [sōla-chaturvēdimaṅgalam].

l.ta: kalikaṭinta man ••• pallūḻiyiṟ ṟennavar kulamakaḷ mānar pā ••••

ṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu tāmar koṭṭattu tāmar nāṭṭup parākkirama
[coḻaccatuppeti maṅkalattup peruṅ]
kil tirumakiḻ ••• kiḻ kūṭṭak kuṟaivaṟak kūṭiyiruntu •••
llaikku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai eṅkaḷḷūr pālan vatikkuk kiḻakkum • •
nāṟpāṟ kellaikkum naṭuvupaṭṭanilam •••• ṟṟu cāṇ kolāl ••••

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 257-B of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the east wall
Rājēndra-Chōḷa II: year 8, day 95: 1060 A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in. It records that the peruṅkuṟi perumakkaḷ of Parākramasōla-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam received gold from the temple treasury and agreed to exempt the
taxes on the land owned by the temple. The produce of the land were to be utilized
for feeding the brāhmaṇas well versed in Vēdas and also for smearing the deity with
oil and other ingredients. The sabhā also agreed to remit the taxes for the temple land.
Sūryabhaṭṭaṉ and others figure as signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. 258 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Rājēndra-Chōḻa II: year 4: 1056 A.D.
inscription.p:
Begins with the praśasti of the king, portion of which are lost. It registers a sale
of 25 vēli of land to the temple of Sri Rudrasōḻai-mahādēva situated in the southern
bank of the river Tiruveḥka at Parākramasōḻa-chatūrvēdimaṅgalam in Dāmar-nāḍu, a
subdivision of Dāmar-koṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, for 40 kāśu by the
assembly of Veṇkuḷaṭṭūr in Kaḻumala-nāḍu, a subdivision of Kāliyūr-kōṭṭam in the
same maṇḍalam. It is also stated that out of 40 kāśu, 10 kāśu, for the price of land
and other 30 kāśu, as deposit for paying tax. The madhyastha Sivakkoḻundu
Chandrasēkaran and others figure as signatories.

yil paṭaikkuṇṭam enṟinnava(ṉ)roṭañciya caḷukkikula (kula)kulai

lotukki kālpaṟittoṭi melkaṭalpāyat turattiya poḻutul

velkoṭi mutal rājapariccinnamuṉ •••••

tu imaṇṭalattut tāmaṟ
koṭṭattu tāmarnāṭṭup parāk

kuṭutta
tu eṅkaḷur kāṭeṟu kollai yippāḻ kiṭa

kāṇiyum itankiḻ oru

innilam ipparicu viṟṟukkuṭuttu itannāl vanta vilaippo[ru*]ḷ
muṟṟum ma[ṟa]

comāciyārum iṟāyūr veṇṇaiyakkiramavittaṉum pāṭakattu comi

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. 259 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara Temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Rājādhirāja-Chōḻa I: Year 30, day 165: 1048 A.D.
inscription.p:
Begins with the praśasti, tiṅgaḷēr taru etc. It records the decision of selling 2000
kuḻi of the common land [ūr-mañjikkam] by the mahāsabhā of Karaikkōṭṭu brahmadēyam
alias Parākramasōḻach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam while they met at the temple of Tiruvāypāḍi
dēva (Krishṇa) situated in the western part of the village, to the temple (Sri Pōndai-
uḍaiyār) probably for providing food offerings (tirumaḍaippaḷḷippuṟam) to the deity
Āḍavallāṉ Parākramasōḻaviṭaṅkar by three individuals namely Mādhavasara bhaṭṭan,
Iḷaiya Nambitta Kramavittaṉ and Iḷaiya Santāna Kramavittaṉ of Seṅgōṭṭuch-chēri. The
temple committee agreed to maintain this charity.

l.ta:
tiṅkaḷer taru tanṟoṅkal veṇkuṭaik kīḻ nilamakaḻ
nilava malarmakaṭ puṇarntu [ceṅ]

kolocci karuṅkali kaṭintu taṉ ciṟiya tātaiyaiyum tiruttamaiyaṉaiyum
kuṟikoḷ taṉṉiḷaṅ [kōkki]

ḷaiyum neṟiyuṇar taṉṟirupputalvar tammaiyum tunṟeḻil vāṉavaṉ
villavaṉ mīṉavaṉ kaṅkaṉ ilaṅkai[yaṟ]

kiṟaivaṉ pulaṅkeḻiṟ pallavaṉ kaṉṉakucciyar kāvalaneṉa poṉṉaṇi
cuṭarmaṇi makuṭañ cūṭṭi paṭar [pukaḻ]

āṅkavaṟ[ka] nāṭaruḷi pāṅkuṟa maṉṉu pallūḻiyuṭ teṉṉavar mūvaruḷ
māṉāparunaṉ poṉmuṭiyāṉā[p paru]

matuṭ pacuntalai porukaḷattarintu vāraḷaviya kaḻal vīrakeraḷaṉai muṉaivaiyiṟ
piṭittu taṉ[āṉaikkiṭuvittu atti]

vāraṇak kaṭakaḷiṟṟālutaippittaruḷi antamil perumpukaṭ cuntara
pāṇṭiyaṉ oṟṟai veṇ[ku]-

ṭaiyum kaṟṟai veṇkavariyum ciṅkātaṉamum veṅkaḷattiḻintu taṉmuṭi
viḻat talaivirittaṭi taḷarṉto[llai]

yil mullaiyūrt turatti olkalil veṇāṭṭaraicaic ceṇāṭṭotukki
mevupukaḻ irāmakuṭamūvar keṭa muṉintu miṭa ke[ḻu]

•• nāṭu viṭṭoṭi kāṭupukko[ḷi*]ppa vañciyam putumalar malaintāṅku
•••

keḻu kāntaḷūrccālai kalamaṟuppittu ākavamallaṉum añca
kevutaṉṟāṅkarum paṭaiyālāṅkavaṉ ceṉaiyuḷ

kaṇṭappaiyaṉum kaṅkātaraṉum vaṇṭamar kaḷiṟṟoṭu maṭiyat tiṇṭiṟal
virutar vikkiyum vicaiyātit-

taṉum tarumuraṭ cāṅkamaiyaṉu mutaliṉar camarapīruvottuṭaiya nimircuṭarp
poṉṉoṭu aiṅkatip puraviyoṭu

piṭittut taṉṉāṭaiyiṟ jayaṅ koṇṭu oṉṉār koḷḷippākkai
uḷḷeri maṭuppittu oru taṉittaṇṭāl po-

mārkali yīḻañ cīriteṉṟeṇṇi uḷaṅkoḷ taṉṉāṭu taṉṉuṟavoṭum
pukuntu viḷaṅkumuṭi kuvitta vīracalāmekaṉ

poṟkaḷattañci kāṟkaḷiṟiḻintu kavvaiyuṟṟoṭi kātaliyoṭun taṉ
ṟavvaiyaip piṭittu tāyai mūkkariya āṅkava-

māṉa nīṅkutaṟkāka mīṭṭu vantu vāṭṭoḻiluḻantu veṅkaḷattulantavac
ciṅkaḷattaraicaṉ poṉṉaṇi muṭiyum kaṉ-

ṉaraṉ vaḻivanturai koḷīḻattaracaṉākiya cirivallavaṉ mataṉarācaṉ
elloḷit taṭamaṇi muṭiyuṅ koṇṭu vaṭapulattu iru

kālāvatum porupaṭai naṭātti taṇṭar tiṉakaraṉ nāraṇaṉ kaṇavati
vaṇṭalar teriyal matucūtaṉaṉeṉa

palavaraicarai muṉaiviya turatti vampalar tarupoḻil kampili nakaruḷ
cāḷukkiyar māḷikait taka[r*]ppittiretamil villavar

mīṉavar veḷaccaḷukkiyar vallavar kocalar paṅkaḷar koṅkaṇar
ciṅkaṇa[raiya]ṇar antira mutalliya aracarṉā-

ṭu niṟaikaḷum āṟiloṉṟavaniyiṭa kūṟukoḷ •••• nāṉmaṟaiyavar
mukantu koḷak kuṭuttu vi-

lo[ka*]ttu viḷaṅka maṉuneṟi ••• ••• ••• jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻaṉ
uyarnta perum pukaḻko-

ṭaiyār tirumaṭaippaḷḷippuram jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa• maṇṭalattu
tāmarkoṭṭattu tāmarnāṭṭu karaikkoṭṭu

var koyilil kūṭṭakkuṟaivaṟa kūṭiyiruntu nammūr
śrīpontai uṭaiyār āti

lukku vaṭakku kolāl nilam kuḻi iraṇṭāyiramum ikkolāl
kuḻi iraṇṭāyiramum tecavatikku kīḻpāṟ kellai kollai ni[lat]

tukku kiḻakku vaṭapāṟkellai viḷainilattukku teṟkum innāṟpāṟ
kellaikku uṭpaṭa naṭuvil kollai nilam kuḻi •••

yiramum patiṉaṟucāṇ kolāl viṟṟu vilaiyāvaṇañ ceytu
kuṭuttom irukālāvatu mukkālāvatu muṟ collappaṭṭa inṉāṟ-

pālellaiyuḷ [iṟaiveṭṭi] kolāl nilam kuḻi iraṇṭāyiramum
vilaiyāvaṇañ ceytu kuṭuttu emmilicaintu vilaip po-

ruḷ muṟṟum aṟakkoṇṭu [i]tuve poruḷmāvaṟutip poruḷ
celavolaiyāvatākavum ituvall(ā)tu veṟu poru-

ḷmāvaṟutip poruḷc celavolai kāṭṭeṉṉap peṟātomākavum
peri koṭṭi viṟṟu vilaiyāvaṇañ ceytu[tan]tom iṉṉilam
vaṟaiyi-

edition.l:
avaikkaṇ mādhavasara bhaṭṭaṉum māṭalampāttu iḷai[ya] nampit
takkiramavittaṉum ceṅkāṭṭucceri iḷaiya [ sandhāna] perumān kirama-

vittanum maṇiyaracan nilam ikkoyilil āṭavalār parākkirama coḻa
viṭaṅkarkku ucciyampotai san

aṟukuṟuṇi irunāḻiyum neyyamutu mucceviṭum kaṟiyamutukku nel
oru ceviṭum āka nel •••

raṇṭukku nellu muvuḻakkum aṭaikkāyamutu veṟuṅkāy nālum

nivantam muṭṭāmai iśrīkoyil vāriyañ ceyvāre aṭuttu •••

•••• tevaṉ ivai eṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. 260 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara Temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Rājādhirāja-Chōḻa I: Year 26, day 120: 1044 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription begins with the praśasti of the king tiṅgaḷēr-taru etc. It records
the decision of the assembly, Parākramasōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Dāmar-nāḍu in
Dāmar-kōṭṭam, which met under a tamarind tree, and decided to sell land as tax-free,
for constructing a watershed for quenching the thirst of king Uḍaiyār Rājēndrachōḻadēva
and his queen Vīramahādēviyār, who probably died at this village, to Sēnāpati
Madurāntakaṉ alias Parākesarivēḷār who was the brother of the queen. The sabhā
which received both the price and tax amount, and agreed to remit the taxes on this
land. The members of the sabhā and an official of kōṭṭam were mentioned.

l.ta:
••• •••• [ti]ṅkaḷer tarutaṉṟoṅkal veṇkuṭaikkiḻ nilamakaḷ nilava
malarkaṭpuṇarntu ceṅkoloccik karuṅkali kaṭi

pa]llūḻiyuṭ ṭeṉṉavar kulamakaṉ māṉāparaṇaṉ poṉmuṭiyāṉāp
paru[maṇip]pacu

ttaṉ kulattavaṉiventaṉ naṉkuṟuta(tai)kaimai araiciyalurimai
varicaiyileyti villavar miṉavar ve-

tu tarā
tala[m]vaḷara jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻaṉ vayaṅku perumpukaḻ (korāja)

taṇṇireri vīram[ā*]tevip pererikku ••••• pākku nilamākavum
viṟṟukkuṭuppa-

tevar ••• yu •••• yolai
tittakkuḷattin vaṭakaraippuḷikkiḻ kūṭṭak kuṟaivaṟak kūṭi ikko-

ṭṭam vakai ceykinṟa [paḻuvūru]ḻāṉ moṭanārāyaṇanuṅ kūṭaviruntu
[icaivu tīṭṭu] eḻuttu maṇaṟpurattu [jeya]nampittak ki[ra*]mavittanum

kīḻpāṟkellai iṟṟiṭaik kālār ellaikku meṟku tenpāṟkellai
śrīpontai iṟaiyiṟuttilā

ppaḻa(ni)lattukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai maṇaṟkunṟukkuk kiḻakkum
vaṭapāṟkellai iṟainilamāna ēttakkiṇattukkut teṟkum a

••• •••• yil naṭuvupaṭṭa nilattāl nīrnilam kuḻi āyirattu aiññūṟṟu
•••• aṟuppukkuḻi eṇṇāyiramum kutaḷu

•• yākat tiruttikkoḷḷa innilam tiruttikkoḷḷap peṟuvāṉāka ••
• nellum ponnum tiruvekampamuṭaiyār [iccīvita]muḷḷiṭṭut
tirukkoṟṟa vā-

calil ponta kuṭimai eppeṟpaṭṭatum veṭṭi yamañciyum ••• •••
eccoṟumaṟṟu ūriṭuvariyum uḷḷiṭṭu maṟṟu mepperppaṭṭa iṟaiyum
nāme kuṭu-

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. 261 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḻa I: Year 48, Māsi, Su. di. (mistake for ba.di.) Monday,
Sittirai: 1118 A.D. Monday, Feb. 11
inscription.p:
Incomplete. This inscription begins with the praśasti of Kulōttuṅgachōḻadēva I
and registers an agreement made by certain Śivabrāhmanas of the temple of Pōndai-
uḍaiyār at Diṉachintāmaṇi-chatūrvēdimaṅgalam in Dāmar-nāḍu, a subdivision of Dāmar-
kōṭṭam, with the Srimāhēsvara-kaṇkāṇiseyvār, kōil-vāriyapperumakkaḷ and the karaṇattār,
for burning two lamps from the interest on gold received from a native of Paruttikkuḍi,
a sub division of Mīseṅgiḷi-nāḍu in Diṉachintāmaṇi-vaḷanāḍu of Sōḻamaṇḍalam. The
names of the temple priests are mentioned in the inscription. Another fragment is also
available at the same place.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilavamalar
makaḷ puṇara uri[m*]aiyil ciṟanta maṇimuṭi cūṭi mīṉavar

ṉilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara ēṉai maṉṉavaririyaluṟṟiḻitarat tikkaṉaittu taṉ
cakaṉaṭā(r)tti vija

ttu ulakuṭaiyāḷoṭum vīṟṟiruntaruḷiya kovirājakecaripaṉmarāṉa
cakkaravarttikaḷ śrīkulottuṅkatevaṟku yāṇṭu nāṟpa-

tteṭṭāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu tāmar koṭṭattu tāmar
nāṭṭu tiṉacintāmaṇi caturvetimaṅkalattu

kṣiṇā
mūrttipaṭṭaṉum tiricciṟṟampala paṭṭaṉum tiruvuṇāyaka paṭṭa-

ṉum cūriyatevapaṭṭaṉum pontai(ntai)ṉāyaka paṭṭaṉum ivvaṉaivom
śrīkoyilil śrīmāhe

vāriyap perumakkaḷukkum karaṇattāṉukkum tīṭṭukkuṭutta paricāvatu
ivvāṇṭu mācimācattu pūrvapakṣattu tiṅkaḷkiḻamaiyum citti-

raiyum peṟṟa nāḷ ••••• coḻamaṇṭalattu cintāmaṇi vaḷanāṭṭu
mī[c]eṅkiḷi nāṭṭu parittikkuṭi parittikkuṭaiyāṉ veḷāṉ koyil
koṇ-

••• ••• •••• poṉ koṇṭu poliyūṭṭāka canti yiraṇṭukku erikkak
kaṭavomāṉa viḷakku iraṇṭukkum kaikkoṇṭa aṉṟāṭu naṟkācu
n[ū*]ṟu ik •••

viḷakku iraṇṭum erikka kaṭavomāka cammatittu ti • •

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. 262 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Rājādhirāja-Chōḻa I: year 34: 1052 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription commences with the praśasti tiṅgaḷērtaru taṉtoṅgal etc. It
records the gift of gold for providing food offering to the deity Pāsupatamūrti when
it was taken out in procession for śrībali in the morning service at the temple of
Pōndai mahādēva, by a Veḷḷāḷa woman named Mādevaṉ Tiruvaṉtāḷ who was a native
of Niyamam in Puḻaṟ-Kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam. The Śivabrahmaṇas of the
temple received the amount and agreed to maintain the charity.

l.ta:
tiṅkaḷertaru taṉṟoṅkal veṇkuṭaikīḻ nilamakaḷ nilava
malarmakaṭ puṇarntu ceṅkolOcci karuṅkali kaṭintu tantaiyār tamaya-

ntampiyar tanṟirumaintar enṟivarai maṇimuṭicūṭṭi kanniy kāvalaṉ
teṉṉavar mūvaruḷ vānakam iruvarukkaruḷi kānakam oruvanukkaḷittup
po-

rucilai ceralan velaikeḻu kāntaḷu[r*]cālai kalammaṟuppittilaṅkaiyark
karacaiyum alaṅkal vallavaṉaiyum kaṉṉakkucciyar kāvala(n)ṉaiyum
ponṉaṇi

muṭittalai taṭintu tan koṭippaṭai ēvi kannāṭakar viṭu kaṭakari puraḷa
tannāṭaiyiṟ tamiḻpparaṇi koṇṭonnār vacciraneṭuvāḷ viccai-

veruneḷittañci ōṭa tan vañciyampaṭaiyālāṅkavan pitāvaiyum
mātāvoṭu catarmali vīṅkunīr pūṇṭūr veñcamattakappaṭuttayaṟ
paṭaiyākavamallaṉ payat-

toṭu varaviṭum oṟṟarai veruvarap piṭippittāṅkavar m(ā)ruvilāhava
mallaṉ yāṅkaṇumañcinanenna nankeḻutic celuttiya pinṉait tan
pulik koṭippaṭaiñarroṭṭa-

[vā]raṇavaraicai kaṭalpuraic ciṟutuṟai peruntuṟai teyva vīmakaci
yenṟu uṟutuṟai mūnṟilum •••• āṅkavarettak kiriyiṉ

mpam eḻilpeṟa niṟutti tankaḻal
paṇintoḻukumaraicarkaḷoṭu ceṉṟāṭi porupuli vīrakkoṭiye
tiyākakkoṭi eṭutte

[on]nalar kava[r*]nta tonnitip piṟakkam iravalarā necarki yīntu viravalil
avanviṭu taṇṭattavan velpuravi nuḷampanuṅ kāḷitāsanum •••

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. 263 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Rājādhirāja-Chōḷa I: Year 32: 1050 A.D.
inscription.p:
The inscription begins with the praśasti of the king ‘tiṅgaḷēr-peravaḷar’ etc. It
records that the assembly Pārākramasōḻa-chatūrvēdimaṅgalam organised a meeting before
the Ayōthi Āḻvar temple and agreed to sell 2000 kuḷi of land after receiving both the
price-money and tax-money for providing food offerings to the god
Tiruchchiṟṟambalamuḍaiyār (Lord Naṭarāja) of Pōndai-uḍaiyār temple. Śri Pōndai Arasu
alias Tiruchchiṟṟambala Porkōil uḍaiyār, a madhyasthā and other members of assembly
figure as signatories.

l.ta: ḷavun tolkulam viḷaṅkat tonṟi malkiya va-

kuṭaticai kaṭāramum taṇṭāṟkoṇṭa tātaitan maṇṭala veṇkuṭai
niḻa-

ṟkiḻt tankuṭai niḻaṟṟa ticaitoṟuñ ceṅkolocci te[n]ṉavan
mān[ā]
paraṇaṉ poṉmuṭi pacuntalai porukaḷattarintu vāraḷaviya kaḻal vīra-

keraḷaṉai ••• •••• āṉaikkiṭuvittu veṇāṭṭa[ra*]cai ceṇāṭṭotukki
pūvakattaracaic ceva-

kīḻaikaḻaniyāl ve-

kola-

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. 264 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Rājēndra-Chōḻa I: year 27: 1039 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription commences with the praśasti of the king “t6irumanni-vaḷara”
etc. It records the gift of money for nūlēru probably for sacred thread used for
hoisting the flag on the beginning day of the festivals, by Miḷaimuḍaiyāṉ Pūvalān
Arumoḻi a native of Puliyūr-nāḍu a subdivision of Arumoḻidēva-vaḻanāḍu in Soḻamaṇdalam
for which a land was purchased by the temple of Pōndai uḍaiya mahādēva at
Parākramasōḷa-chatūrvēdimaṇgalam and which was made tax-free by mahāsabhā. Another
gift of gold (10(3/4) kaḻañju and 6 mañjāḍi) for a lamp by Umādēviyār, wife of
Vallavaraiyar Vandyadēvār was also recorded. It mentions the water sources like
Kundvaip-pērēri, Sundarasōḻap-pērēri, Paramēśvara-vāykkāl and Kīḻkōṭṭaiyūrvati while
describing the boundaries.

l.ta:
tirumannivaḷara irunila maṭantaiyum poṟceyappāvaiyum
cīrttaṉic celviyum taṉperunteviyarāki iṉpuṟa neṭutuyil

cūḻmatiḷ koḷḷipākkaiyum naṇṇaṟkaru muraṇmaṇṇaik kaṭakkamum

peruma-

edition.l:
cūṟṟi paṉaṅkāṭaṉ pūmi kuḻi 1[5]0 meṟpaṭi ērikiḻ meṟku paramecuvara
vāykkālukku vaṭakku 1 kaṇṭattu kāḷattu niṉṟa caṉmaṉ bhūmi
kuḻi 20- m śrīcuntaracoḻapereri kiḻ koṭṭaiyūr vatikkuk
kiḻakku parameśvara vākkālukku vaṭakku 1 kaṇṭattu maṅkalattu
virapattiraṉ bhūmi kuḻi 250 kuḻiyum ākakkuḻi 600m cantirātittavaṟ
iṟaiyiṟuttuk kuṭuppomāṉom ivvūr sabhaiyom kara •• ūr
20 ṉāl uṭaiyār vallavaraiyar vandyadēvar nampirāṭṭiyār umādēvi-

vaṟku tirunantā viḷakkoṉṟinukku
vaitta pon patiṉ mukkaḻaiñce āṟu mañcāṭi ippoṉ
patiṉmukkaḻaiñce ••• ••• •••• ca

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 265 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall of a maṇḍapa in a field adjoining the
same temple
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḻa III: year 3: 1181 A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. It registers the gift of eight vēli of land at Karaikōṭṭu-Brahmadēsam
alias Dīnachintāmaṇi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Dāmar-nāḍu, a subdivision of Dāmar-
kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam, by Uḻḻāvūr Kamban Vānadaraiyan which was his
jīvitakkāṇi to the temple of Sri Pōndaiyuḍaiyār. Kaḍappēri and Sambuvarāyap-pērāru
are mentioned. Kamban Vāṇadaraiyan might have been a headman of Dīnachintāmaṇich-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. 266 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Chandramauḷīśvara temple-on the north wall
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḻa III: year 27: 1205 A.D.
inscription.p:
It registers the gift of land called Iruṅgōḷa Viḷagām to meet the cost of
constructing a gōpura, providing food offerings, burning lamp and performing sacred
bath by Āḍavallān Gaṅgaikoṇḍān alias Iruṅgōḷan.

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. 267 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District
Kalyāṇavarada temple-on the stones built into the wall
Rājādhirāja-Chōḻa: •••: 11th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragments. The King name Rājādhirāja deva and a temple name Āditya-griha
are alone survived

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. 268 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Selliyammaṉ temple-on the stones lying in the courtyard
11th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragments in four pieces contains the names of village and territorial divisions
such as Mayipālakulakālapperunderu, Sōlakulavallinallūr, Sōmaṅgalam, Maṇaṟpākkuḍi,
Dāmar-nāḍu and kōṭṭam in [Jayaṅ]goṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam, Pākkai-nāḍu in Gangaikoṇda-
sōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, other details are lost.

l.ta:
•••• koṇṭu itukku itu ipporuḷ māvaṟutip poruḷ cilave
••••
••• mavittan eḻuttu ippaṭik[ku*] ivai pākkattu ī

ip •••

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. 269 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, (Pudūr) Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Rudrakōṭīśvara temple-on the west and north wall of the central shrine
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḻa I: Year 21: 1091 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription begins with the praśasti of the king Pugaḻ sūḻnta puṇari etc.
It records the exemption of taxes for the dēvadāna lands of Sri Rudrasōlai-mahādēva by
the sabhā of Dinachintāmaṉi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam of Dāmar-nāḍu, a sub-division of
Dāmark-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅkonḍasōla-maṇḍalam.

The dēvadāna lands were located in twelve different places, purchased in the
12th, 14th and 21st years of the king, from twelve different persons. The boundaries
of the lands and measurements were mentioned in detail. Among the boundries of
the land, a parcel of land owned by the medicine man, Tiruvikrama bhaṭṭaṉ as
vaidya-bhōgam is found mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. 270 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam, (Pudūr) Cheyyār Taluk, North Arcot District.
Rudrakōṭīśvara temple-on the north wall of the central shrine
Rājēndra-Chōḻa 11: Year 9: 1061 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription begins as Chaṇḍēsvaran ōlai and with the praśasti, ‘Tirumātar
puviyeṉṉum’ etc. It records the public sale of land to the temple of Śri Rudrasōlai
mahādēva situated on the southern bank of the river Veḥka by the sabhā of
Karaikkōṭṭu-brahmadēyam alias Parākarmasōḻach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Dāmar-kōṭṭam
in Jayaṅgonḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam. It is also stated that the said land measuring 10,000 kuḻi
was utilized for the settlement of the peasants of the Rudrasōlai mahādēva temple in
the name of Alaṅkāravalli chēri.

l.ta: ḻil caḷukki p[o*]ruta veḻattai viṭātu ke-
ma meṟṟina veñcina varaica roṭina vāṟṟa talai[ñ]-
yum variya teviyum varākavel koṭi mutal[rā]-

ramum tevar pūmikku vaṭakkum vaṭakiḻakku cār nila varappataṟku
ki[ḻ*]pāṟkellai kollai nilattukku me[ṟ*]kum tenpārkellai
it(ta)tevar pūmikku vaṭakkum

melpāṟkellaiy eṅkaḷ kollai nilattukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai
eṅkaḷ kollai nilattukku teṟkuminnāṉkellai-

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 271 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam (Pudūr), Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Rudrakōṭīsvara temple-on the north wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the same shrine
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḻa II: year 3: 1136 A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. It begins with the praśasti, Pūmēvu vaḷar etc. and registers the
royal grant of Anapāyanallūr which was separated from Rājēndrasōḻanallūr (inclusive of
Avvēlur and Veṇkarpāḍi) as a dēvadāna to the deity Sri Rudrasōlai uḍaiya Mahādēva
at Karaikkōṭṭu Brahmadēsam alias Dīnachintāmaṇich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Dāmar-
nāḍu, a sub division of Dāmark-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅkoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam. It is stated that
as the temple did not have any dēvadāna land previously, the order was made at the
instance of Kulōttuṅgasōḻa Savarnādhirājaṉ. When the king was seated on the thrown
in the abhīsheka maṇḍapa of his palace at Vikramachōḻapuram.

l.ta:
pūmevu vaḷar tirupponmārvu puṇara nāmevu kalaimakaḷ
nilamperutu ciṟappa vicayamāmakaḷ
velpuyat tiruppa icai[vi]n celva eṇṭicai viḷaṅka niruparva

rntu cīr vaḷartarum aṇimuṭi kavitteṉa maṇimuṭicūṭi mallaiñālattup pala •

oṉpatemukkāle iraṇṭu mākkāṇi araikkāṇi muntirikaik kiḻoṉpatu
māvaraiyinā ••• ••• •••

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. 272 of 1915.)
Brahmadēsam (Pudūr), Cheyyar Taluk, North Arcot District.
Rudrakōṭīsvara temple-on the east wall of the same maṇḍapa
Śri Vīrakumāra Kampaṇa-Uḍaiyār: Śaka 1285, Sobhakrit, Dhanus,
su. di. 11, Wednesday, Punarvasu: 1363 A.D.
inscription.p:
It registers the gift of taxes for burning 3 perpetual lamps to the temple of Sri
Rudrasōlai Uḍaiyār at Karaikkōṭṭu Brahmadēsam in Dāmal-nāḍu in Jayaṅkoṇḍasōḷa-
maṇḍalam. The gift was made on the order of Pōvindarasar, the agent of the
Mahāpradhāṉi Sōmaiya Daṇṇāyaka.

l.ta: ka vaiytta tirununtāviḷakku muṉṟukku viṭṭa śrīmaṉma-

taṅkaḷ tirumaṭai viḷākatti •••• kku vāṇiyar ceṉaiaṅkāṭiyaḷ
civaṉpaṭavar eṭuttinaṭattumper maṟṟum ittirumaṭaivi-

āykkuṭi makkaḷ uḷḷa perkkum
āyam uḷḷāyam nūlāyam nāṭ ••••• cammātam maṟṟum āyam
nokki varum epperppaṭṭa pala upātiyum utaka

iṟaiili āka kuṭuttom ittaṉmattukku virotam
paṇṇiṉavaṉ keṅ ••••• ḷḷuvāṉ itu

edition.p:
Below this inscription there are two bits I & II which belong to 16, 17th
century.
This inscription belongs to 11th c. A.D. characters.

l.ta: ••• ra muṟṟamappeṟu putiyitu uḷḷiṭṭa pala nellāyaṅ •

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(A.R. No. 273 of 1915.)
Kaḍalūr, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
Agnīśvara temple-on the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine
Kīlaga, Āḍi, day 10: 15th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of tax (uḷḷāyam) amount collected from Kaḍalūr
for burning a lamp in the temple of Tiruvagnīsvaramuḍaiya nāyanār by Śrivīrasiru
Girināthaṉ, a chief (Manḍalīsvaran)

l.ta: intat tanmattukku akitam paṇṇinavan keṅkaikkaraiyil ka

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. 274 of 1915.)
Kaḍalūr, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District
On detached stones built into the same shrine
Jat. Sundara-Pāṇḍyan: year 17: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the remission of tax amount by Muṉaiyadaṟaiyaṉ in
favour of the temple of Kulasēkhara Sōḷīśvaramuḍaiyār of Pallavaṉ-paṭṭinam.

l.ta: ṉār tirumaṭaiviḷākam tiruccuṟṟālaiyāṉa iṭaṅkaḷil irukkum niyā-

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. 275 of 1915.)
Kaḍalūr, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District
Agnīśvara temple-on a stone built into the roof of the same maṇḍapa
13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
It records that this roof stones were the gift of Vānavaṉmādēvi Uḍaiyāṉ
Teṉṉavadaraiyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. 276 of 1915.)
Kaḍalūr, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District
Agnīśvara temple-on a stone lying in the court-yard
Achyutadēva-Mahārāya: Vikriti, Magara: 16th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of the village Attipiravaḍai alias Namaśivāyanallūr
for the offerings and worship for the merit of Irumaḍi Tirumalai-Nāyaka at the temple
of Tiruvakkisuramuḍaiya-Tambirānār in Kaḍalūr on the first day of the year (i.e)
Chittirai (mahā-saṅkrama) by Jegatappi Koṇḍirāja and Nāchchiyana, according to the
order of Namaśivāya nāyakkar. (see below 280)

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. 277 of 1915.)
Kaḍalūr, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District
Agnīśvara temple-on another stone in the same place
Achyutadēva-Mahārāya: Kāḷayukti: 16th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription is in verse composed in nērisai veṇbā metre. It registers a gift
of 1000 kuḻi of land at Kaḍalūr to the temple of Akkiyāṇḍār for performing morning
service by the poet Agaḷaṅgaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. 278 of 1915.)
Kaḍalūr, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
Agnīsvara temple-on a few detached stones in the same place
Rājarāja-Chōlā I: year lost: 10th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
It mentions Paḻampaṭṭanam, and a temple Tirukkaṇṇisvaram, other details are
lost.

l.ta:
le iṟai iṟuppatākavum niṉṟa ni •••• vom •• ṉeṉṟa poḻuṅ[kuḻi]kku
i ••••

ttuviṭṭa nilattāl ••• ••• ••• iṟai ēṟṟik •••• ceyta nilattāl
iṟai koḷvatāka

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. 279 of 1915.)
Kaḍalūr, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
Agnīsvara temple-on some detached stones in the same place
10-11th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragments. Probably belongs to the reign of Rājarāja I. Registers the gift of
gold (1/8 poṉ) for burning perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirukaṇṭīsvaradēvar at
Paḻampaṭṭaṇam. Nāḍanārai and his younger brother Irēvaṉ accepted to burn the lamp
after receiving the gold. The next inscription refers to another gift of land (1050 kuḻi)
for food offering to Āḍavallār (Naṭarāja), in the temple of Tirukaṇṭīśvaram. The
inscription also records Vaikānasan Gōvindan Selvaṉ and another official Ādanūr
uḍaiyāṉ.

l.ta:
••••• ṭṭina nāṭṭup paḻampaṭṭaṇattu śrīkoyiluṭai[ya*] na •• vāḷateva

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. 280 of 1915.)
Kaḍalūr, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District
Agnīsvara temple-on five other detached stones lying in the same place
Achyutadēva-Mahārāya: ••• : 16th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of village [Attippidavai Namasivaya]nallur for performing
worship on the occation of Makarasaṅkrama in the temple of Tiruvakkisvaramuḍaiya
Tambirānār, for the merit of Irumaḍi Tirumalai Nāyakkar under the instruction of
Nāmaśivāya Nāyakkar by certain Jagatappi Koṇḍirāsa, Kaḍaikkūṭṭu Nāchchiyaṇar. (See
above 276 of 1915).

l.ta:
makārāyarkku cellā niṉṟa vikuṟuti varuṣam makaracaṅkiṟama puṇṇiya
kālatti[le]-

rumaṭi tirumalai nāyakkar taṉmamāka namacivāya nāyakkar appiṇaipaṭik[ku]
[ceka]-

tāppi koṇṭirācāvum kaṭaikkūṭṭu nācciaṇarum attippiṭavai
namacivāya •••• ṭanallūrki-

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. 281 of 1915.)
Kaḍalūr, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
Agnīsvara temple-on five other detached stones lying in the same place
Pārthivēṉdravarman: year 11: 970 A.D.
inscription.p:
In three fragments. The first one seems to record the gift of lamp to the temple
by the members of the great mercantile committee [Mahā-nagaram]. It states that the
accountant named Dāmōdaranādittaṉ, a Vaikhānasa wrote this document. The other
two refer to a gift of land to the Kaṇṇisvaramuḍaiya Mahādēva, for providing various
offerings and services.

l.ta: aṭaikkāyamutu iraṇṭukkum ilaiyamutu eṭṭukkumāka nellu uriyum

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. 282 of 1915.)
Kaḍalūr, Cuddalore Taluk, South Arcot District.
Agnīsvara temple-on a stone lying in a field near the same village
14th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
It registers a gift of two hundred kuḷi of land by the ūrār to the maṭha called
Tiruvāsagaṉ-tirumaḍam.

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. 283 of 1915.)
Vikravāṇḍi, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
Bhuvanēśvara Temple-on the north wall of the central shrine
Koṇḍurayyamarāsar: •••: Yuva, Paṅguṉi, Day 5: 16-17th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
This is much damaged and seems to be a gift of land to certain Āchchārya, by
the ūrār.

l.ta:
vum koṇṭurayyama rācar ••• yarāḷukku •••
••• ••• ḻa[ūro]ṉa ••• ācāriyarkku •••
ceṅkāṭṭu paṟ[ṟā]yina paṟṟum paṟṟaṭai puravum samata

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. 284 of 1915.)
Vikravāṇḍi, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District
Bhuvanēśvara Temple-on the north wall of the central shrine
Sadāsivarāya: Saka 14[. .]: 16th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Badly damaged and fragmentary. Seems to refer some settlement at the temple
complex and the fixation of rent per year.

l.ta: inta maṭaiviḷākattile e ••• •••• yatta oru varuṣa ••• •••••

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. 285 of 1915.)
Vikravāṇḍi, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
Bhuvanēśvara temple-on the north, west and south walls of the central shrine
Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa I: year 32: 1102 A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragment. This inscription records the gift for a perpetual lamp to the temple
of Chēdikulachintāmaṇi-Īśvaramuḍaiyār at Gaṅgaikoṉḍach-chōḻapuram by a merchant
of this village named Sēntanūrāḻvaṉ Moḍanallūr Nakkaṉ alias Aṟivaikaḷ Kaṟpaka Vēḷāṉ.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava
malar]makaḷ puṇara [urimaiyiṟ ciṟanta maṇi] muṭicūṭi miṉavar nilaikeṭa
villavar kulai[tara] eṉaimaṉṉa ••••

nūrāḻv[ā*]ṉ moṭanallūr nakkaṉāṉa aṟivaikaḷ kaṟpakaveḷāṉ
iṉnakarattār pakkal koṇṭu ārkkaṭṭukki •••

edition.p:
* Two lines of a fragmentary inscription of the 10th century characters are incised
above this inscription, which reads:

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. 285-A of 1915.)
Vikravāṇḍi, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
Bhuvanēśvara temple-on the north, west and south walls of the central shrine
12th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragments. The first one, record the steps taken by certain Karuvuṇṇayakar and
Araisukkaḍiyār to compensate the loss of certain copper utensils (seppukkiḍāram and
seppukkuḍam) of this temple, which were originally gifted by the queen. The second
one records the gift of 20 kāsu for burning two perpetual lamps in that temple. This
gift was received by the Śivabrāhmaṇās and they undertook the responsibility of
burning those lamps.

l.ta:
ṟku yāṇṭu •• āvatu kaṅkaikoṇṭacoḻa vaḷaṉāṭṭu puṟaiyūrnā[ṭṭu*]
nakaram puvaṉimāṇikkapurattuc cetikula[ci*]

va ••• •••• ḻvāriṭṭa ce[p]pukkiṭāramuñ ceppukkuṭamuṅ
keṭṭamaiyil ikkoyilukku

rañ ceykiṟa
karuvuṇāyakarum araicukkaṭiyārum ikkoyilil civa

ttavaṟ cella erikkak
kaṭavomānom ikkoyilil civa

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. 286 of 1915.)
Vikravāṇḍi, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
Bhuvanēśvara temple-on the north, west and south walls
of the central shrine
Vikrama-Chōḷa: year 4: 1122 A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. This record contains only the praśasti of the king and his regnal
year.

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. 287 of 1915.)
Vikravāṇḍi, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District
Bhuvanēśvara temple-on the north, west and south walls of the central shrine
Vikrama-Chōḻa: year lost: 12th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragments. Contains only the historical introduction of the king. It seems to
be a record of the gift of land as tirunondāviḷakkup-puṟam for burning perpetual lamp
in the temple by a certain Parigrahattār.

l.ta:
pūmātupuṇarap puvimātu [vaḷara] nāmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu

vaḷarppa kaliṅkamiriyak kaṭal ••• ••• niḻaṟṟi vira

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. 288 of 1915.)
Vikravāṇḍi, Villuppuram Taluk, South Arcot District
Bhuvanēśvara temple-on a stone setup in the courtyard of the same temple
Rājanārāyaṇa-Sambuvarāya: •••: 14th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
It records the exemption of taxes on the temple land, (tirunāmattukkāṇi) and
given as sarvamānya to the god Chēdikulachintāma[ṇī]śuramuḍaiya nāyanār at
Vikkiramapāṇḍiyanallūr by the King.

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. 289 of 1915.)
Vikravāṇḍi, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District.
Varadarāja Perumāḷ Temple—on the north and west walls of the central shrine
Vīra-Narasimharāya: Śaka 1431, Sukla, Kaṟkaṭaka, su.di. 5, Friday,
Hasta: 1509 A.D. Sunday (seems to be wrong), July 22
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of a village which was a hamlet of Vikkirapāṇḍi
in Vīḍūr-Paṟṟu, in Oymān-vaḷanāḍu, in Palkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam, a sub-division of Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-
maṇḍalam to the temple of Varadarāja Perumāḷ at Vikkiramapāṇḍi as tirunāmattukkāṇi
by a feudatory Tribhuvanavīrakulaveṉṉa Samma[ṭṭi] Pottiyadēva Sōḻamahārājaṉ.

l.ta:
pāṣaikkut
tappuvarāyira kaṇṭaṉ muvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭa

malaik

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. 290 of 1915.)
Vikravāṇḍi, Villupuram Taluk, South Arcot District
Agastyēśvara Temple-on the east and north walls of the central shrine
Immaḍi Veṅkaḍapati dēva-Mahārāya: Tai, 11, Uttarāshādha: 16th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Built both in middle and at the end. Only name of the king and
certain Veṅkayya Nāyakkar are noticed.

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. 567 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koyil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
Madhyasthanātha temple-on the north and west walls of the central shrine and the west
wall of the mukhamaṇḍapa.
Iṟandakālameḍutta perumāḷ Śrīvallabhadēva: year 7, Śaka 1463, piḷava,
Āvaṇi, 22, su. di. 7, Tuesday, Anusha: 1541 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of one kuḻi and eight mā of land freed from taxes
at Paṭṭakuṟichchi in Āri-nāḍu, to Kaṇdēru Sōmanātha Bhaṭṭa for compiling pañchāṅga
(calendar)

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. 568 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
Madhyasthanātha temple-on the same walls of the same place
Parākrama-Pāṇḍya: year 5, Saka 1470, Kilaka, Āvaṇi, 17; su. di.
Tiruvōṇam.: 1548 A.D. Thursday, Aug. 16
inscription.p:
Incomplete. It stops with the name of the village Mannākkuḍi in Chōḻa
maṇḍalam. It seems to record the gift made by the king to a certain Rudravīṇai
Vīraiyar.

l.ta:
m peṟṟa tiruvoṇattiṉ nāḷ
coḻamaṇṭala maṉṉaṉākuṭi

nāḷ jātaṉāṉa naṅkumāraṉ tirunelveli
perumāḷ nam koyilil •••••

•• vatukku tiruvāḻiyāka kaṟpittu vara viṭukaiyil ōlaippaṭi kuṭukkak
kaṟpitta koṭṭūr nāṭṭu kāmantatevaṉ muṉ āḷai ••••

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. 569 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
Madhyasthanātha temple-on the south wall of the central shrine
King name and year lost: Kaṉṉi, 1, su. di [4]: 16th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in and incomplete. It records the grant of land to a certain Vikkiramasiṅgadēvar
and his progenies with the stipulation that they have to measure out certain amount
of paddy and other items to the god at Malaiyaḍikuṟichchi, other details are lost.

l.ta: vikkumiṭattu payir pārttu pāḻuc[āvi] nikki payir •••
kuṟuṇi kalamāka mā[t*]tāl nellu eḻukalamum kuṟuṇi •••
innilattil eḷḷu varaku tiṉai pullu cāmi viḷainta nilattu ••••

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. 570 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelveli District
Madhyasthanātha temple-on the south wall of the central shrine
Jaṭ. Konērinmaikoṇḍān Abhirāma Sri Perūmaḷ
Kulasēkhara Perūmaḷ Parākrama Pāṇḍya: year 7, Saka 1471, 13 ba. di. 7, Sunday,
Pushya: 1549 A.D. Sunday, Oct. 13
inscription.p: Incomplete. This record ends with the name of the King and date.

l.ta:
konermaikoṇṭāṉ apirāma
parākkiṟamapāṇṭiyateva nantanarāṉa śrī perumāḷ ku[laceka[ra*] perumāḷ
parākkira]mapāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. 571 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Saṅkaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
Madhyasthanātha Temple-on the south wall of the central shrine
Jaṭ. Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍān: Saka 1462, Sārvari, Tai, 12,
su. di•••: 1541 A.D. Saturday, Jan. 8.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and built in at the right end. Seems to record some gift to one
Tiruvagni Bhaṭṭa of Gāyatri gōtra and Bōdhāyaṉa sūtra by prince Tirunelvēlipperumāḷ.

l.ta:
ṉ mel cellāniṉṟa cārvari varuṣam
tai mā

kkum perumāḷ tiruvakkinipaṭṭaṉukku naṅkumāraṉ tirunelvelip
perumāḷ naṅkoyil kāṇiyā[y]-^1^

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. 572 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinarkoil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
Madhyasthanātha Temple-at the entrance of the mukhamaṇḍapa in front of the same
shrine: right side.
Jaṭ. Kulasēkara-Pāṇḍya: year lost: 16th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in at the left end. Records the gift of 25 sheep and also a hanging lamp
weighing 25 (?) by a shepherd Bhūtan Peṟṟān for burning a twilight lamp to the deity
at Malaiyaḍikuruchchi, a southern hamlet, of Śri Pirāntakach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 573 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
Madhyasthanātha Temple-at the entrance of the mukhamaṇḍapa in front of the same
shrine: right side.
Jaṭ. Kulasēkara-Pāṇḍya: Year 12: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription registers a gift of 50 sheep for a perpetual lamp by
Aḻagiyāṉ Araisu alias Kaṇḍiyadēvaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. 574 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
Madhyasthanātha Temple-at the entrance of the mukhamaṇḍapa in front of the
same shrine: right side.
Mār. Vikrama-Pāṇḍya: Year 4, Āvani: 13th C. A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers a gift of 11 sheep to burn a twilight lamp in the
temple of Piṇakkaṟukkunāyanār by one Pāḍikkōṉ Pallavaraiyaṉ.

edition.l:
ṉacca[kkaravattikaḷ] śrī vikkiramapāṇṭiya [dēva]ṟkku yāṇṭu 4- vatu āvaṇi
māsattu piṇakkaṟukku nāya-

l.ta: ṉāṟ[kku ca] ••••• ṅku ••••• āḷvāṉāṉa pāṭikkoṉ pallavaraiyaṉ

vaitta cantiyātipa viḷakku oṉṟukku iṭṭa āṭu patiṉṉoṉṟum
cantirāti(va)ttavaṟ celvatāka[vum] ••••

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. 575 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
Madhyasthanātha Temple—at the entrance into the mukhamaṇḍapa in front of the
same shrine: right side.
Jaṭ. Kulasēkara-Pāṇḍya: Year 11: 13th C.A.D.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of 50 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp and also a lamp stand
to the temple of Piṇakkaṟukkum Mahādēvar at Malaiyaḍikkuṟichchi, a hamlet of Śrī
Parāntaka chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēyam in Āri-nāḍu by Ariyān Āndān alias
Vānakōvaraiyaṉ Pallavaraiyaṉ of Maṇapaḍaivīḍu, northern hamlet of Śri Va[l*]lavamaṅgalam
in Kīḷkarai-Kūṟṟam.

l.ta:
karai kūṟṟattu śrī va[l*]lavamaṅkalattu vaṭapiṭākai maṇapaṭaiviṭṭi
[liruk]kum • yāṉ ariyāṉ āṇṭāṉ

āṉa vāṇakovarai[yaṉ*] pallavaraiyaṉ ittevaṟkku ivāṇṭai
tai māta mutal

ai[mpatu] cāvā muvā
perāṭu ākavum i viḷakku eriya nilaiviḷakku oṉṟu ||-

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 576 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
Madhyasthanātha temple—at the entrance into the mukhamaṇḍapa; left side
Jaṭ. Kulasēkara-Pāṇḍya: year 13+3: 1253 A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of 50 sheep for a perpetual lamp to the temple
of Piṇakkaṟukku Mahādēva at Malaiyaḍikkuṟichi, a southern hamlet of Śri Parāntaka
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēyam in Āri-nāḍu by Veḷḷāḷaṉ Paṭṭaṉ of Peruṅgulai in
Sōṇāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 577 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelvēli District.
Madhyasthanātha Temple-on the entrance into the mukha-maṇḍapa: left side
Aḻagaṉ Perumāḷ Ativīrarāma Śri Vallabha Pāṇḍya: Year 9, Saka 1493,
Prajōtpatti, Uttarāyaṇa, Śaiśira-ritu, Kumbha-ravi, 16, Su. di. 10, Friday, subha-yōga,
subha-karaṇa, “Ārdra”: 1571 A.D. Friday, Feb. 22
inscription.p:
Damaged at the end. Begins with the eulogy of the King. It records the gift of
land as sarvamānya in the village Vaḍakuṟuchchi in Āriyā-nāḍu to the temple of
Piṇakkaṟukku Nāyanār by the king. Koḍumalūruḍaiyāṉ figuars as signatory.

edition.l: viṉānu |— śubhamastatu §

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 578 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
Madhyasthanātha temple-at the entrance into the mukha-maṇḍapa: left side
Jaṭ. Kulasēkara-Pāṇḍya: year 13+1, Karkaṭaka, 27, Rēvati,
Friday: 1266 A.D.
inscription.p:
It records the gift of one āṉai-achchu for a lamp to the temple of Piṇakkaṟukku
Nāyanār at Malaiyaḍikkuṟichchi, a southern hamlet of Śri Parāntakach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam,
a brahmadēya in Āri-nāḍu by Kēsava Nārāyaṇaṉ, a native of Māṅkōṭṭuch-chēri in
Malai-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
mā[ṇṭu*] kaṟkaṭakanāyaṟṟu irupatteḻu tiyatiyum irevatiyum peṟṟa
veḷḷikkiḻamaināḷ ārināṭṭu

kaccatuppeti maṅkalattu teṉpiṭākai malaiyaṭikkuṟicci uṭaiyār
piṇakkaṟukku nāyaṉāṟku [malai]maṇṭalattu māṅ-

[potuvil] civa
tuvom āka kaikkoṇṭa āṉai accu oṉṟu §

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 579 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koyil Taluk, Tirunelveli District.
Madhyasthanātha temple—at the entrance into the mukha-maṇḍapa: left side
Kulasēkara-Pāṇḍya: year 13+9: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. It registers a gift for a twilight lamp to the temple of Piṇakk[arukk*]um
Nāyanār at Malaiyaḍikkuṟichchi, a southern hamlet of Śri Parāntakach-chaturvēḍimaṅgalam,
a brahmadēya in Āri-nāḍu by Nārāyaṉaṉ Mādhavaṉ, a native of a village in Purisai-
nāḍu, a subdivision of Malai-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
piṇak[kaṟuk*]kum nāya(ṉ)ṉāṟkum malaimaṇṭalattu puricaināṭṭu puṟattu
nārāyaṇaṉ mātavaṉ[ṉeṉ] nāṭa[ṉ]vait-

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. 580 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelveli District
Madhyasthanātha temple-at the entrance into the mukhamaṇḍapa: left side
Māṟ. Sundara-Pāṇḍya: Year 11, Āvaṇi: 13th c. A.D.
inscription.p:
It records the gift of money (one achchu) for a twilight lamp to the temple of
Piṇakkaṟukku nāyanār at Malaiyaḍikkuṟichchi, a southern hamlet of Śri Parāntakach-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Āri-nāḍu by Dēvaṉ Śrīkumaran, native of
Orutāḻaipaḷḷi in Malai-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
maṅkalattu teṉpiṭākai malaiyaṭaikkuṟicciyil
uṭaiyār piṇakkaṟukkunāyaṉāṟku malaimaṇṭalattu oru tā-

ivviḷakku akaṉāḻikaip potuvāka erippatāṉamaikku [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. 581 of 1915.)
Dārukāpuram, Sankaranayinar koil Taluk, Tirunelveli District
Madhyasthanātha temple-on the north wall of the same maṇḍapa
Jaṭ. Kulasēkara-Pāṇḍya: Year 13+4: 1207 A.D.
inscription.p:
Begins with the praśasti of the king Pūvin kiḻatti etc. This inscription records
the exemption of the taxes such as kaḍamai, antarāyam, silvari, and peruvari on four
vēli of land by the king who sent his order to the mahasabhā of Pirāntaka-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Āri-nāḍu. The land had been previously sold
to the temple of Piṇakkaṟutta nāyanār at Sri Parantaka-chaturvedimaṅgalam by the
above sabhā who were now ordered by the king to exempt the taxes on this land.
Several persons figures as signatories.
